easypaperless

easypaperless — Python API wrapper for paperless-ngx.

 1"""easypaperless — Python API wrapper for paperless-ngx."""
 2
 3import logging
 4
 5from easypaperless.client import PaperlessClient
 6from easypaperless.exceptions import (
 7    AuthError,
 8    NotFoundError,
 9    PaperlessError,
10    ServerError,
11    TaskTimeoutError,
12    UploadError,
13    ValidationError,
14)
15from easypaperless.models import (
16    Correspondent,
17    CustomField,
18    CustomFieldValue,
19    Document,
20    DocumentMetadata,
21    DocumentNote,
22    DocumentType,
23    FieldDataType,
24    FileMetadataEntry,
25    SearchHit,
26    StoragePath,
27    Tag,
28    Task,
29    TaskStatus,
30)
31from easypaperless.store import DocumentStore
32from easypaperless.sync import SyncPaperlessClient
33
34logging.getLogger("easypaperless").addHandler(logging.NullHandler())
35

37    # Clients
38    "PaperlessClient",
39    "SyncPaperlessClient",
40    "DocumentStore",
41    # Models
42    "Correspondent",
43    "CustomField",
44    "CustomFieldValue",
45    "Document",
46    "DocumentMetadata",

class PaperlessClient: 
  40class PaperlessClient:
  41    """Async client for the paperless-ngx API.
  42
  43    All operations are flat methods on the client.  String names are resolved
  44    to integer IDs automatically wherever the API requires IDs (tags,
  45    correspondents, document types, storage paths).
  46
  47    Use as an async context manager to ensure the underlying HTTP connection
  48    pool is closed when you are done:
  49
  50    Example:
  51        async with PaperlessClient(url="http://localhost:8000", api_key="abc") as client:
  52            docs = await client.list_documents()
  53    """
  54
  55    def __init__(
  56        self,
  57        url: str,
  58        api_key: str,
  59        *,
  60        poll_interval: float = 2.0,
  61        poll_timeout: float = 60.0,
  62    ) -> None:
  63        """Create an async paperless-ngx client.
  64
  65        Args:
  66            url: Base URL of the paperless-ngx instance
  67                (e.g. ``"http://localhost:8000"``).
  68            api_key: API token.  Generate one in paperless-ngx under
  69                *Settings → API → Generate Token*.
  70            poll_interval: Seconds between status checks when ``wait=True``
  71                is passed to :meth:`upload_document`.  Default: ``2.0``.
  72            poll_timeout: Maximum seconds to wait for a document to finish
  73                processing before raising
  74                :exc:`~easypaperless.exceptions.TaskTimeoutError`.
  75                Default: ``60.0``.
  76        """
  77        self._session = HttpSession(base_url=url, api_key=api_key)
  78        self._resolver = NameResolver(self._session)
  79        self._poll_interval = poll_interval
  80        self._poll_timeout = poll_timeout
  81
  82    async def close(self) -> None:
  83        """Close the underlying HTTP connection pool.
  84
  85        Called automatically when used as an async context manager.
  86        """
  87        await self._session.close()
  88
  89    async def __aenter__(self) -> PaperlessClient:
  90        return self
  91
  92    async def __aexit__(self, *args: Any) -> None:
  93        await self.close()
  94
  95    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
  96    # Documents
  97    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
  98
  99    async def get_document(self, id: int, *, include_metadata: bool = False) -> Document:
 100        """Fetch a single document by its ID.
 101
 102        Args:
 103            id: Numeric paperless-ngx document ID.
 104            include_metadata: When ``True``, the extended file-level metadata
 105                (checksums, sizes, MIME type) is fetched concurrently from
 106                ``/documents/{id}/metadata/`` and attached to
 107                :attr:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document.metadata`.
 108                Default: ``False``.
 109
 110        Returns:
 111            The :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document` with the
 112            given ID.  When ``include_metadata=True`` the ``metadata``
 113            attribute is populated; otherwise it is ``None``.
 114
 115        Raises:
 116            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists
 117                with that ID.
 118        """
 119        if include_metadata:
 120            doc_resp, meta_resp = await asyncio.gather(
 121                self._session.get(f"/documents/{id}/"),
 122                self._session.get(f"/documents/{id}/metadata/"),
 123            )
 124            data = doc_resp.json()
 125            data["metadata"] = meta_resp.json()
 126        else:
 127            resp = await self._session.get(f"/documents/{id}/")
 128            data = resp.json()
 129        return Document.model_validate(data)
 130
 131    async def get_document_metadata(self, id: int) -> DocumentMetadata:
 132        """Fetch the extended file-level metadata for a document.
 133
 134        This is a lower-overhead alternative to
 135        ``get_document(id, include_metadata=True)`` when you only need the
 136        metadata and not the full document object.
 137
 138        Args:
 139            id: Numeric paperless-ngx document ID.
 140
 141        Returns:
 142            A :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.DocumentMetadata`
 143            instance containing checksums, sizes, MIME type, and embedded
 144            file metadata.
 145
 146        Raises:
 147            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists
 148                with that ID.
 149        """
 150        resp = await self._session.get(f"/documents/{id}/metadata/")
 151        return DocumentMetadata.model_validate(resp.json())
 152
 153    async def list_documents(
 154        self,
 155        *,
 156        search: str | None = None,
 157        search_mode: str = "title_or_text",
 158        tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
 159        any_tag: list[int | str] | None = None,
 160        exclude_tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
 161        correspondent: int | str | None = None,
 162        any_correspondent: list[int | str] | None = None,
 163        exclude_correspondents: list[int | str] | None = None,
 164        document_type: int | str | None = None,
 165        any_document_type: list[int | str] | None = None,
 166        exclude_document_types: list[int | str] | None = None,
 167        asn: int | None = None,
 168        created_after: str | None = None,
 169        created_before: str | None = None,
 170        added_after: str | None = None,
 171        added_before: str | None = None,
 172        modified_after: str | None = None,
 173        modified_before: str | None = None,
 174        checksum: str | None = None,
 175        page_size: int = 25,
 176        page: int | None = None,
 177        ordering: str | None = None,
 178        descending: bool = False,
 179        max_results: int | None = None,
 180    ) -> list[Document]:
 181        """Return a filtered list of documents.
 182
 183        All tag, correspondent, and document-type parameters accept either
 184        integer IDs or string names — names are resolved to IDs transparently.
 185
 186        Args:
 187            search: Search string.  Behaviour depends on ``search_mode``.
 188            search_mode: How ``search`` is applied.  One of:
 189
 190                * ``"title_or_text"`` *(default)* — full-text search across
 191                  title and OCR content (Whoosh FTS, raw API ``search``
 192                  parameter).
 193                * ``"title"`` — case-insensitive substring match on title
 194                  only (raw API ``title__icontains``).
 195                * ``"query"`` — paperless query language, e.g.
 196                  ``"tag:invoice date:[2024 TO *]"`` (raw API ``query``).
 197                * ``"original_filename"`` — case-insensitive substring match
 198                  on the original file name (raw API
 199                  ``original_filename__icontains``).
 200
 201            tags: Documents must have **all** of these tags (AND semantics).
 202                Accepts tag IDs or tag names.
 203            any_tag: Documents must have **at least one** of these tags
 204                (OR semantics).  Accepts tag IDs or tag names.
 205            exclude_tags: Documents must have **none** of these tags.
 206                Accepts tag IDs or tag names.
 207            correspondent: Filter to documents assigned to exactly this
 208                correspondent.  Accepts a correspondent ID or name.
 209            any_correspondent: Filter to documents assigned to **any** of
 210                these correspondents (OR semantics).  Accepts IDs or names.
 211                Takes precedence over ``correspondent`` when both are given.
 212            exclude_correspondents: Exclude documents assigned to any of
 213                these correspondents.  Accepts IDs or names.
 214            document_type: Filter to documents of exactly this type.
 215                Accepts a document-type ID or name.
 216            any_document_type: Filter to documents whose type is **any** of
 217                these (OR semantics).  Accepts IDs or names.  Takes
 218                precedence over ``document_type`` when both are given.
 219            exclude_document_types: Exclude documents whose type is any of
 220                these.  Accepts IDs or names.
 221            asn: Filter by archive serial number.
 222            created_after: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
 223                documents created **after** this date are returned.
 224            created_before: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
 225                documents created **before** this date are returned.
 226            added_after: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
 227                documents **added** (ingested) after this date are returned.
 228            added_before: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
 229                documents **added** (ingested) before this date are returned.
 230            modified_after: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
 231                documents **modified** after this date are returned.
 232            modified_before: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
 233                documents **modified** before this date are returned.
 234            checksum: MD5 checksum of the original file (exact match).
 235                Returns only the document whose original file matches this
 236                checksum.
 237            page_size: Number of results per API page.  Default: ``25``.
 238                Increase to reduce the number of HTTP requests for large
 239                result sets.
 240            page: Return only this specific page of results (1-based). When
 241                set, auto-pagination is disabled and only the results from
 242                that single page are returned. Use together with ``page_size``
 243                to control page size. Default: ``None`` (auto-paginate through
 244                all pages).
 245            ordering: Field name to sort by. Examples: ``"created"``,
 246                ``"title"``, ``"added"``. Use together with ``descending`` to
 247                control direction. Default: ``None`` (server default ordering).
 248            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
 249                ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
 250                Default: ``False``.
 251            max_results: Stop fetching pages once this many documents have
 252                been collected and return at most this many results.
 253                ``None`` *(default)* returns everything.
 254
 255        Returns:
 256            List of :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`
 257            objects.
 258        """
 259        params: dict[str, Any] = {"page_size": page_size}
 260
 261        if search is not None:
 262            api_param = _SEARCH_MODE_MAP.get(search_mode, "search")
 263            params[api_param] = search
 264
 265        if tags is not None:
 266            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", tags)
 267            params["tags__id__all"] = ",".join(str(t) for t in resolved)
 268
 269        if any_tag is not None:
 270            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", any_tag)
 271            params["tags__id__in"] = ",".join(str(t) for t in resolved)
 272
 273        if exclude_tags is not None:
 274            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", exclude_tags)
 275            params["tags__id__none"] = ",".join(str(t) for t in resolved)
 276
 277        if any_correspondent is not None:
 278            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("correspondents", any_correspondent)
 279            params["correspondent__id__in"] = ",".join(str(c) for c in resolved)
 280        elif correspondent is not None:
 281            resolved_id = await self._resolver.resolve("correspondents", correspondent)
 282            params["correspondent__id__in"] = resolved_id
 283
 284        if exclude_correspondents is not None:
 285            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("correspondents", exclude_correspondents)
 286            params["correspondent__id__none"] = ",".join(str(c) for c in resolved)
 287
 288        if any_document_type is not None:
 289            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("document_types", any_document_type)
 290            params["document_type__id__in"] = ",".join(str(d) for d in resolved)
 291        elif document_type is not None:
 292            resolved_id = await self._resolver.resolve("document_types", document_type)
 293            params["document_type"] = resolved_id
 294
 295        if exclude_document_types is not None:
 296            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("document_types", exclude_document_types)
 297            params["document_type__id__none"] = ",".join(str(d) for d in resolved)
 298
 299        if asn is not None:
 300            params["archive_serial_number"] = asn
 301
 302        if created_after is not None:
 303            params["created__date__gt"] = created_after
 304
 305        if created_before is not None:
 306            params["created__date__lt"] = created_before
 307
 308        if added_after is not None:
 309            params["added__date__gt"] = added_after
 310
 311        if added_before is not None:
 312            params["added__date__lt"] = added_before
 313
 314        if modified_after is not None:
 315            params["modified__date__gt"] = modified_after
 316
 317        if modified_before is not None:
 318            params["modified__date__lt"] = modified_before
 319
 320        if checksum is not None:
 321            params["checksum"] = checksum
 322
 323        if ordering is not None:
 324            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
 325
 326        if page is not None:
 327            params["page"] = page
 328            resp = await self._session.get("/documents/", params=params)
 329            items = resp.json().get("results", [])
 330            if max_results is not None:
 331                items = items[:max_results]
 332            return [Document.model_validate(item) for item in items]
 333
 334        items = await self._session.get_all_pages("/documents/", params, max_results=max_results)
 335        return [Document.model_validate(item) for item in items]
 336
 337    async def update_document(
 338        self,
 339        id: int,
 340        *,
 341        title: str | None = None,
 342        date: str | None = None,
 343        correspondent: int | str | None = None,
 344        document_type: int | str | None = None,
 345        storage_path: int | str | None = None,
 346        tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
 347        asn: int | None = None,
 348        custom_fields: list[dict] | None = None,
 349    ) -> Document:
 350        """Partially update a document (PATCH semantics — only passed fields change).
 351
 352        Args:
 353            id: Numeric ID of the document to update.
 354            title: New document title.
 355            date: Creation date as an ISO-8601 string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).
 356            correspondent: Correspondent to assign, as an ID or name.
 357                Pass ``0`` to clear.
 358            document_type: Document type to assign, as an ID or name.
 359                Pass ``0`` to clear.
 360            storage_path: Storage path to assign, as an ID or name.
 361                Pass ``0`` to clear.
 362            tags: Full replacement list of tags (IDs or names).  The existing
 363                tag list is replaced, not merged.
 364            asn: Archive serial number to assign.
 365            custom_fields: List of ``{"field": <field_id>, "value": ...}``
 366                dicts.  Replaces the existing custom-field values.
 367
 368        Returns:
 369            The updated :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`.
 370        """
 371        logger.debug("Updating document id=%d", id)
 372        payload: dict[str, Any] = {}
 373
 374        if title is not None:
 375            payload["title"] = title
 376        if date is not None:
 377            payload["created"] = date
 378        if correspondent is not None:
 379            payload["correspondent"] = await self._resolver.resolve("correspondents", correspondent)
 380        if document_type is not None:
 381            payload["document_type"] = await self._resolver.resolve("document_types", document_type)
 382        if storage_path is not None:
 383            payload["storage_path"] = await self._resolver.resolve("storage_paths", storage_path)
 384        if tags is not None:
 385            payload["tags"] = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", tags)
 386        if asn is not None:
 387            payload["archive_serial_number"] = asn
 388        if custom_fields is not None:
 389            payload["custom_fields"] = custom_fields
 390
 391        resp = await self._session.patch(f"/documents/{id}/", json=payload)
 392        return Document.model_validate(resp.json())
 393
 394    async def delete_document(self, id: int) -> None:
 395        """Permanently delete a document.
 396
 397        Args:
 398            id: Numeric ID of the document to delete.
 399
 400        Raises:
 401            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists
 402                with that ID.
 403        """
 404        logger.debug("Deleting document id=%d", id)
 405        await self._session.delete(f"/documents/{id}/")
 406
 407    async def download_document(self, id: int, *, original: bool = False) -> bytes:
 408        """Download the binary content of a document.
 409
 410        Args:
 411            id: Numeric ID of the document to download.
 412            original: If ``False`` *(default)*, returns the archived
 413                (post-processed) PDF.  If ``True``, returns the original
 414                file that was uploaded.
 415
 416        Returns:
 417            Raw file bytes.
 418        """
 419        endpoint = "download" if original else "archive"
 420        resp = await self._session.get_download(f"/documents/{id}/{endpoint}/")
 421        content_type = resp.headers.get("content-type", "")
 422        if "text/html" in content_type or resp.content[:9].lower().startswith(b"<!doctype"):
 423            raise ServerError(
 424                f"Download returned an HTML page (content-type: {content_type!r}). "
 425                "The server redirected to a login page even after re-attaching auth. "
 426                "Run with --verbose to see redirect details.",
 427                status_code=None,
 428            )
 429        return resp.content
 430
 431    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 432    # Upload
 433    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 434
 435    async def upload_document(
 436        self,
 437        file: str | Path,
 438        *,
 439        title: str | None = None,
 440        created: str | None = None,
 441        correspondent: int | str | None = None,
 442        document_type: int | str | None = None,
 443        storage_path: int | str | None = None,
 444        tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
 445        asn: int | None = None,
 446        wait: bool = False,
 447        poll_interval: float | None = None,
 448        poll_timeout: float | None = None,
 449    ) -> str | Document:
 450        """Upload a document to paperless-ngx.
 451
 452        Args:
 453            file: Path to the file to upload (``str`` or
 454                :class:`~pathlib.Path`).
 455            title: Title to assign to the document.  Paperless-ngx derives
 456                one from the file name if omitted.
 457            created: Creation date as an ISO-8601 string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).
 458            correspondent: Correspondent to assign, as an ID or name.
 459            document_type: Document type to assign, as an ID or name.
 460            storage_path: Storage path to assign, as an ID or name.
 461            tags: Tags to assign, as IDs or names.
 462            asn: Archive serial number to assign.
 463            wait: If ``False`` *(default)*, returns immediately with the
 464                task ID string.  If ``True``, polls the task-status endpoint
 465                until processing completes and returns the resulting
 466                :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`.
 467            poll_interval: Override the client-level ``poll_interval`` for
 468                this upload.  Only used when ``wait=True``.
 469            poll_timeout: Override the client-level ``poll_timeout`` for
 470                this upload.  Only used when ``wait=True``.
 471
 472        Returns:
 473            The Celery task ID string when ``wait=False``, or the fully
 474            processed :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`
 475            when ``wait=True``.
 476
 477        Raises:
 478            ~easypaperless.exceptions.UploadError: If paperless-ngx reports
 479                that the processing task failed.
 480            ~easypaperless.exceptions.TaskTimeoutError: If ``wait=True`` and
 481                processing does not complete within the timeout.
 482        """
 483        file_path = Path(file)
 484        file_bytes = file_path.read_bytes()
 485        logger.info("Uploading %r (%d bytes)", file_path.name, len(file_bytes))
 486
 487        data: dict[str, Any] = {}
 488        if title is not None:
 489            data["title"] = title
 490        if created is not None:
 491            data["created"] = created
 492        if correspondent is not None:
 493            data["correspondent"] = await self._resolver.resolve("correspondents", correspondent)
 494        if document_type is not None:
 495            data["document_type"] = await self._resolver.resolve("document_types", document_type)
 496        if storage_path is not None:
 497            data["storage_path"] = await self._resolver.resolve("storage_paths", storage_path)
 498        if tags is not None:
 499            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", tags)
 500            data["tags"] = resolved
 501        if asn is not None:
 502            data["archive_serial_number"] = asn
 503
 504        files = {"document": (file_path.name, file_bytes)}
 505        resp = await self._session.post("/documents/post_document/", data=data, files=files)
 506        task_id: str = resp.text.strip('"')
 507        logger.debug("Upload accepted, task_id=%r", task_id)
 508
 509        if not wait:
 510            return task_id
 511
 512        interval = poll_interval if poll_interval is not None else self._poll_interval
 513        timeout = poll_timeout if poll_timeout is not None else self._poll_timeout
 514        return await self._poll_task(task_id, poll_interval=interval, poll_timeout=timeout)
 515
 516    async def _poll_task(
 517        self, task_id: str, *, poll_interval: float, poll_timeout: float
 518    ) -> Document:
 519        start = time.monotonic()
 520        deadline = start + poll_timeout
 521        while time.monotonic() < deadline:
 522            resp = await self._session.get("/tasks/", params={"task_id": task_id})
 523            tasks = resp.json()
 524            if not tasks:
 525                await asyncio.sleep(poll_interval)
 526                continue
 527
 528            task = Task.model_validate(tasks[0])
 529            elapsed = time.monotonic() - start
 530            logger.debug("Polling task %r (status=%s, elapsed=%.1fs)", task_id, task.status.value, elapsed)
 531            if task.status == TaskStatus.SUCCESS:
 532                doc_id = int(task.related_document)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
 533                logger.info("Task %r succeeded, document_id=%d", task_id, doc_id)
 534                return await self.get_document(doc_id)
 535            elif task.status == TaskStatus.FAILURE:
 536                logger.warning("Task %r failed: %s", task_id, task.result)
 537                raise UploadError(f"Document processing failed: {task.result}")
 538            await asyncio.sleep(poll_interval)
 539
 540        elapsed = time.monotonic() - start
 541        logger.warning("Task %r timed out after %.1fs", task_id, elapsed)
 542        raise TaskTimeoutError(f"Task {task_id!r} did not complete within {poll_timeout}s")
 543
 544    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 545    # Bulk operations
 546    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 547
 548    async def bulk_edit(
 549        self, document_ids: list[int], method: str, **parameters: Any
 550    ) -> None:
 551        """Execute a bulk-edit operation on a list of documents.
 552
 553        This is a low-level method; prefer the higher-level helpers such as
 554        :meth:`bulk_add_tag`, :meth:`bulk_remove_tag`, and
 555        :meth:`bulk_modify_tags`.
 556
 557        Args:
 558            document_ids: List of document IDs to operate on.
 559            method: Bulk-edit method name as recognised by paperless-ngx
 560                (e.g. ``"add_tag"``, ``"remove_tag"``, ``"delete"``).
 561            **parameters: Additional keyword arguments forwarded to the API as
 562                the ``parameters`` object.
 563        """
 564        payload = {"documents": document_ids, "method": method, "parameters": parameters}
 565        await self._session.post("/documents/bulk_edit/", json=payload)
 566
 567    async def bulk_add_tag(self, document_ids: list[int], tag: int | str) -> None:
 568        """Add a tag to multiple documents in a single request.
 569
 570        Args:
 571            document_ids: List of document IDs to tag.
 572            tag: Tag to add, as an ID or name.
 573        """
 574        tag_id = await self._resolver.resolve("tags", tag)
 575        await self.bulk_edit(document_ids, "add_tag", tag=tag_id)
 576
 577    async def bulk_remove_tag(self, document_ids: list[int], tag: int | str) -> None:
 578        """Remove a tag from multiple documents in a single request.
 579
 580        Args:
 581            document_ids: List of document IDs to un-tag.
 582            tag: Tag to remove, as an ID or name.
 583        """
 584        tag_id = await self._resolver.resolve("tags", tag)
 585        await self.bulk_edit(document_ids, "remove_tag", tag=tag_id)
 586
 587    async def bulk_modify_tags(
 588        self,
 589        document_ids: list[int],
 590        *,
 591        add_tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
 592        remove_tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
 593    ) -> None:
 594        """Add and/or remove tags on multiple documents atomically.
 595
 596        Args:
 597            document_ids: List of document IDs to modify.
 598            add_tags: Tags to add, as IDs or names.
 599            remove_tags: Tags to remove, as IDs or names.
 600        """
 601        add_ids = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", add_tags or [])
 602        remove_ids = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", remove_tags or [])
 603        await self.bulk_edit(document_ids, "modify_tags", add_tags=add_ids, remove_tags=remove_ids)
 604
 605    async def bulk_delete(self, document_ids: list[int]) -> None:
 606        """Permanently delete multiple documents in a single request.
 607
 608        Args:
 609            document_ids: List of document IDs to delete.
 610        """
 611        await self.bulk_edit(document_ids, "delete")
 612
 613    async def bulk_edit_objects(
 614        self,
 615        object_type: str,
 616        object_ids: list[int],
 617        operation: str,
 618        **parameters: Any,
 619    ) -> None:
 620        """Execute a bulk operation on non-document objects (tags, etc.).
 621
 622        Args:
 623            object_type: The paperless-ngx object type string (e.g.
 624                ``"tags"``, ``"correspondents"``).
 625            object_ids: List of object IDs to operate on.
 626            operation: Operation name recognised by the
 627                ``/bulk_edit_objects/`` endpoint.
 628            **parameters: Additional keyword arguments forwarded directly to
 629                the API payload.
 630        """
 631        payload = {
 632            "objects": object_ids,
 633            "object_type": object_type,
 634            "operation": operation,
 635            **parameters,
 636        }
 637        await self._session.post("/bulk_edit_objects/", json=payload)
 638
 639    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 640    # Internal resource helpers
 641    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 642
 643    async def _list_resource(
 644        self,
 645        resource: str,
 646        model_class: type,
 647        params: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
 648    ) -> list:
 649        if params and "page" in params:
 650            resp = await self._session.get(f"/{resource}/", params=params)
 651            items = resp.json().get("results", [])
 652        else:
 653            items = await self._session.get_all_pages(f"/{resource}/", params)
 654        return [model_class.model_validate(item) for item in items]
 655
 656    async def _get_resource(self, resource: str, id: int, model_class: type):
 657        resp = await self._session.get(f"/{resource}/{id}/")
 658        return model_class.model_validate(resp.json())
 659
 660    async def _create_resource(self, resource: str, model_class: type, **kwargs: Any):
 661        logger.debug("Creating %s resource", resource)
 662        payload = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if v is not None}
 663        resp = await self._session.post(f"/{resource}/", json=payload)
 664        self._resolver.invalidate(resource)
 665        return model_class.model_validate(resp.json())
 666
 667    async def _update_resource(self, resource: str, id: int, model_class: type, **kwargs: Any):
 668        logger.debug("Updating %s resource id=%d", resource, id)
 669        payload = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if v is not None}
 670        resp = await self._session.patch(f"/{resource}/{id}/", json=payload)
 671        self._resolver.invalidate(resource)
 672        return model_class.model_validate(resp.json())
 673
 674    async def _delete_resource(self, resource: str, id: int) -> None:
 675        logger.debug("Deleting %s resource id=%d", resource, id)
 676        await self._session.delete(f"/{resource}/{id}/")
 677        self._resolver.invalidate(resource)
 678
 679    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 680    # Tags
 681    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 682
 683    async def list_tags(
 684        self,
 685        *,
 686        ids: list[int] | None = None,
 687        name_contains: str | None = None,
 688        page: int | None = None,
 689        page_size: int | None = None,
 690        ordering: str | None = None,
 691        descending: bool = False,
 692    ) -> list[Tag]:
 693        """Return tags defined in paperless-ngx.
 694
 695        Args:
 696            ids: Return only tags whose ID is in this list.
 697            name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on tag name
 698                (raw API ``name__icontains``).
 699            page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables
 700                auto-pagination. Default: ``None`` (return all).
 701            page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server
 702                default is used.
 703            ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: ``"name"``, ``"id"``.
 704            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
 705                ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
 706
 707        Returns:
 708            List of :class:`~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag` objects.
 709        """
 710        params: dict[str, Any] = {}
 711        if ids is not None:
 712            params["id__in"] = ",".join(str(i) for i in ids)
 713        if name_contains is not None:
 714            params["name__icontains"] = name_contains
 715        if page is not None:
 716            params["page"] = page
 717        if page_size is not None:
 718            params["page_size"] = page_size
 719        if ordering is not None:
 720            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
 721        return await self._list_resource("tags", Tag, params or None)
 722
 723    async def get_tag(self, id: int) -> Tag:
 724        """Fetch a single tag by its ID.
 725
 726        Args:
 727            id: Numeric tag ID.
 728
 729        Returns:
 730            The :class:`~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag` with the given ID.
 731
 732        Raises:
 733            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no tag exists with
 734                that ID.
 735        """
 736        return await self._get_resource("tags", id, Tag)
 737
 738    async def create_tag(
 739        self,
 740        *,
 741        name: str,
 742        color: str | None = None,
 743        is_inbox_tag: bool | None = None,
 744        match: str | None = None,
 745        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
 746        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
 747        parent: int | None = None,
 748    ) -> Tag:
 749        """Create a new tag.
 750
 751        Args:
 752            name: Tag name. Must be unique.
 753            color: Background colour in the paperless-ngx UI as a CSS hex
 754                string (e.g. ``"#ff0000"``).
 755            is_inbox_tag: When ``True``, newly ingested documents receive this
 756                tag automatically until processed. At most one tag should be
 757                the inbox tag.
 758            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
 759                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
 760            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
 761                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
 762                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
 763            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
 764                case-insensitively.
 765            parent: ID of an existing tag to use as parent, enabling
 766                hierarchical tag trees. ``None`` creates a root-level tag.
 767
 768        Returns:
 769            The newly created :class:`~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag`.
 770        """
 771        return await self._create_resource(
 772            "tags",
 773            Tag,
 774            name=name,
 775            color=color,
 776            is_inbox_tag=is_inbox_tag,
 777            match=match,
 778            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
 779            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
 780            parent=parent,
 781        )
 782
 783    async def update_tag(
 784        self,
 785        id: int,
 786        *,
 787        name: str | None = None,
 788        color: str | None = None,
 789        is_inbox_tag: bool | None = None,
 790        match: str | None = None,
 791        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
 792        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
 793        parent: int | None = None,
 794    ) -> Tag:
 795        """Partially update a tag (PATCH semantics).
 796
 797        Args:
 798            id: Numeric ID of the tag to update.
 799            name: Tag name. Must be unique.
 800            color: Background colour in the paperless-ngx UI as a CSS hex
 801                string (e.g. ``"#00ff00"``).
 802            is_inbox_tag: When ``True``, newly ingested documents receive this
 803                tag automatically until processed. At most one tag should be
 804                the inbox tag.
 805            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
 806                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
 807            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
 808                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
 809                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
 810            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
 811                case-insensitively.
 812            parent: ID of an existing tag to use as parent, enabling
 813                hierarchical tag trees.
 814
 815        Returns:
 816            The updated :class:`~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag`.
 817        """
 818        return await self._update_resource(
 819            "tags",
 820            id,
 821            Tag,
 822            name=name,
 823            color=color,
 824            is_inbox_tag=is_inbox_tag,
 825            match=match,
 826            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
 827            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
 828            parent=parent,
 829        )
 830
 831    async def delete_tag(self, id: int) -> None:
 832        """Delete a tag.
 833
 834        Args:
 835            id: Numeric ID of the tag to delete.
 836
 837        Raises:
 838            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no tag exists with
 839                that ID.
 840        """
 841        await self._delete_resource("tags", id)
 842
 843    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 844    # Correspondents
 845    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 846
 847    async def list_correspondents(
 848        self,
 849        *,
 850        ids: list[int] | None = None,
 851        name_contains: str | None = None,
 852        page: int | None = None,
 853        page_size: int | None = None,
 854        ordering: str | None = None,
 855        descending: bool = False,
 856    ) -> list[Correspondent]:
 857        """Return correspondents defined in paperless-ngx.
 858
 859        Args:
 860            ids: Return only correspondents whose ID is in this list.
 861            name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on correspondent
 862                name (raw API ``name__icontains``).
 863            page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables
 864                auto-pagination. Default: ``None`` (return all).
 865            page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server
 866                default is used.
 867            ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: ``"name"``, ``"id"``.
 868            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
 869                ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
 870
 871        Returns:
 872            List of
 873            :class:`~easypaperless.models.correspondents.Correspondent`
 874            objects.
 875        """
 876        params: dict[str, Any] = {}
 877        if ids is not None:
 878            params["id__in"] = ",".join(str(i) for i in ids)
 879        if name_contains is not None:
 880            params["name__icontains"] = name_contains
 881        if page is not None:
 882            params["page"] = page
 883        if page_size is not None:
 884            params["page_size"] = page_size
 885        if ordering is not None:
 886            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
 887        return await self._list_resource("correspondents", Correspondent, params or None)
 888
 889    async def get_correspondent(self, id: int) -> Correspondent:
 890        """Fetch a single correspondent by its ID.
 891
 892        Args:
 893            id: Numeric correspondent ID.
 894
 895        Returns:
 896            The :class:`~easypaperless.models.correspondents.Correspondent`
 897            with the given ID.
 898
 899        Raises:
 900            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no correspondent
 901                exists with that ID.
 902        """
 903        return await self._get_resource("correspondents", id, Correspondent)
 904
 905    async def create_correspondent(
 906        self,
 907        *,
 908        name: str,
 909        match: str | None = None,
 910        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
 911        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
 912    ) -> Correspondent:
 913        """Create a new correspondent.
 914
 915        Args:
 916            name: Correspondent name (sender/recipient). Must be unique.
 917            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
 918                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
 919            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
 920                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
 921                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
 922            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
 923                case-insensitively.
 924
 925        Returns:
 926            The newly created
 927            :class:`~easypaperless.models.correspondents.Correspondent`.
 928        """
 929        return await self._create_resource(
 930            "correspondents",
 931            Correspondent,
 932            name=name,
 933            match=match,
 934            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
 935            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
 936        )
 937
 938    async def update_correspondent(
 939        self,
 940        id: int,
 941        *,
 942        name: str | None = None,
 943        match: str | None = None,
 944        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
 945        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
 946    ) -> Correspondent:
 947        """Partially update a correspondent (PATCH semantics).
 948
 949        Args:
 950            id: Numeric ID of the correspondent to update.
 951            name: Correspondent name (sender/recipient). Must be unique.
 952            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
 953                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
 954            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
 955                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
 956                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
 957            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
 958                case-insensitively.
 959
 960        Returns:
 961            The updated
 962            :class:`~easypaperless.models.correspondents.Correspondent`.
 963        """
 964        return await self._update_resource(
 965            "correspondents",
 966            id,
 967            Correspondent,
 968            name=name,
 969            match=match,
 970            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
 971            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
 972        )
 973
 974    async def delete_correspondent(self, id: int) -> None:
 975        """Delete a correspondent.
 976
 977        Args:
 978            id: Numeric ID of the correspondent to delete.
 979
 980        Raises:
 981            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no correspondent
 982                exists with that ID.
 983        """
 984        await self._delete_resource("correspondents", id)
 985
 986    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 987    # Document Types
 988    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 989
 990    async def list_document_types(
 991        self,
 992        *,
 993        ids: list[int] | None = None,
 994        name_contains: str | None = None,
 995        page: int | None = None,
 996        page_size: int | None = None,
 997        ordering: str | None = None,
 998        descending: bool = False,
 999    ) -> list[DocumentType]:
1000        """Return document types defined in paperless-ngx.
1001
1002        Args:
1003            ids: Return only document types whose ID is in this list.
1004            name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on document-type
1005                name (raw API ``name__icontains``).
1006            page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables
1007                auto-pagination. Default: ``None`` (return all).
1008            page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server
1009                default is used.
1010            ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: ``"name"``, ``"id"``.
1011            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
1012                ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
1013
1014        Returns:
1015            List of
1016            :class:`~easypaperless.models.document_types.DocumentType`
1017            objects.
1018        """
1019        params: dict[str, Any] = {}
1020        if ids is not None:
1021            params["id__in"] = ",".join(str(i) for i in ids)
1022        if name_contains is not None:
1023            params["name__icontains"] = name_contains
1024        if page is not None:
1025            params["page"] = page
1026        if page_size is not None:
1027            params["page_size"] = page_size
1028        if ordering is not None:
1029            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
1030        return await self._list_resource("document_types", DocumentType, params or None)
1031
1032    async def get_document_type(self, id: int) -> DocumentType:
1033        """Fetch a single document type by its ID.
1034
1035        Args:
1036            id: Numeric document-type ID.
1037
1038        Returns:
1039            The :class:`~easypaperless.models.document_types.DocumentType`
1040            with the given ID.
1041
1042        Raises:
1043            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document type
1044                exists with that ID.
1045        """
1046        return await self._get_resource("document_types", id, DocumentType)
1047
1048    async def create_document_type(
1049        self,
1050        *,
1051        name: str,
1052        match: str | None = None,
1053        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
1054        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
1055    ) -> DocumentType:
1056        """Create a new document type.
1057
1058        Args:
1059            name: Document-type name (e.g. ``"Invoice"``, ``"Receipt"``).
1060                Must be unique.
1061            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
1062                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
1063            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
1064                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
1065                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
1066            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
1067                case-insensitively.
1068
1069        Returns:
1070            The newly created
1071            :class:`~easypaperless.models.document_types.DocumentType`.
1072        """
1073        return await self._create_resource(
1074            "document_types",
1075            DocumentType,
1076            name=name,
1077            match=match,
1078            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
1079            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
1080        )
1081
1082    async def update_document_type(
1083        self,
1084        id: int,
1085        *,
1086        name: str | None = None,
1087        match: str | None = None,
1088        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
1089        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
1090    ) -> DocumentType:
1091        """Partially update a document type (PATCH semantics).
1092
1093        Args:
1094            id: Numeric ID of the document type to update.
1095            name: Document-type name (e.g. ``"Invoice"``, ``"Receipt"``).
1096                Must be unique.
1097            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
1098                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
1099            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
1100                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
1101                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
1102            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
1103                case-insensitively.
1104
1105        Returns:
1106            The updated
1107            :class:`~easypaperless.models.document_types.DocumentType`.
1108        """
1109        return await self._update_resource(
1110            "document_types",
1111            id,
1112            DocumentType,
1113            name=name,
1114            match=match,
1115            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
1116            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
1117        )
1118
1119    async def delete_document_type(self, id: int) -> None:
1120        """Delete a document type.
1121
1122        Args:
1123            id: Numeric ID of the document type to delete.
1124
1125        Raises:
1126            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document type
1127                exists with that ID.
1128        """
1129        await self._delete_resource("document_types", id)
1130
1131    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
1132    # Storage Paths
1133    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
1134
1135    async def list_storage_paths(
1136        self,
1137        *,
1138        ids: list[int] | None = None,
1139        name_contains: str | None = None,
1140        page: int | None = None,
1141        page_size: int | None = None,
1142        ordering: str | None = None,
1143        descending: bool = False,
1144    ) -> list[StoragePath]:
1145        """Return storage paths defined in paperless-ngx.
1146
1147        Args:
1148            ids: Return only storage paths whose ID is in this list.
1149            name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on storage-path
1150                name (raw API ``name__icontains``).
1151            page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables
1152                auto-pagination. Default: ``None`` (return all).
1153            page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server
1154                default is used.
1155            ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: ``"name"``, ``"id"``.
1156            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
1157                ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
1158
1159        Returns:
1160            List of
1161            :class:`~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath`
1162            objects.
1163        """
1164        params: dict[str, Any] = {}
1165        if ids is not None:
1166            params["id__in"] = ",".join(str(i) for i in ids)
1167        if name_contains is not None:
1168            params["name__icontains"] = name_contains
1169        if page is not None:
1170            params["page"] = page
1171        if page_size is not None:
1172            params["page_size"] = page_size
1173        if ordering is not None:
1174            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
1175        return await self._list_resource("storage_paths", StoragePath, params or None)
1176
1177    async def get_storage_path(self, id: int) -> StoragePath:
1178        """Fetch a single storage path by its ID.
1179
1180        Args:
1181            id: Numeric storage-path ID.
1182
1183        Returns:
1184            The :class:`~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath`
1185            with the given ID.
1186
1187        Raises:
1188            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no storage path
1189                exists with that ID.
1190        """
1191        return await self._get_resource("storage_paths", id, StoragePath)
1192
1193    async def create_storage_path(
1194        self,
1195        *,
1196        name: str,
1197        path: str | None = None,
1198        match: str | None = None,
1199        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
1200        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
1201    ) -> StoragePath:
1202        """Create a new storage path.
1203
1204        Args:
1205            name: Storage-path name. Must be unique.
1206            path: Template string for the archive file path. Supported
1207                placeholders: ``{created_year}``, ``{created_month}``,
1208                ``{created_day}``, ``{correspondent}``, ``{document_type}``,
1209                ``{title}``, ``{asn}``. Example:
1210                ``"{created_year}/{correspondent}/{title}"``. When omitted,
1211                the server default location is used.
1212            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
1213                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
1214            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
1215                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
1216                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
1217            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
1218                case-insensitively.
1219
1220        Returns:
1221            The newly created
1222            :class:`~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath`.
1223        """
1224        return await self._create_resource(
1225            "storage_paths",
1226            StoragePath,
1227            name=name,
1228            path=path,
1229            match=match,
1230            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
1231            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
1232        )
1233
1234    async def update_storage_path(
1235        self,
1236        id: int,
1237        *,
1238        name: str | None = None,
1239        path: str | None = None,
1240        match: str | None = None,
1241        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
1242        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
1243    ) -> StoragePath:
1244        """Partially update a storage path (PATCH semantics).
1245
1246        Args:
1247            id: Numeric ID of the storage path to update.
1248            name: Storage-path name. Must be unique.
1249            path: Template string for the archive file path. Supported
1250                placeholders: ``{created_year}``, ``{created_month}``,
1251                ``{created_day}``, ``{correspondent}``, ``{document_type}``,
1252                ``{title}``, ``{asn}``. Example: ``"{title}"``.
1253            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
1254                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
1255            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
1256                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
1257                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
1258            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
1259                case-insensitively.
1260
1261        Returns:
1262            The updated
1263            :class:`~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath`.
1264        """
1265        return await self._update_resource(
1266            "storage_paths",
1267            id,
1268            StoragePath,
1269            name=name,
1270            path=path,
1271            match=match,
1272            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
1273            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
1274        )
1275
1276    async def delete_storage_path(self, id: int) -> None:
1277        """Delete a storage path.
1278
1279        Args:
1280            id: Numeric ID of the storage path to delete.
1281
1282        Raises:
1283            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no storage path
1284                exists with that ID.
1285        """
1286        await self._delete_resource("storage_paths", id)
1287
1288    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
1289    # Custom Fields
1290    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
1291
1292    async def list_custom_fields(
1293        self,
1294        *,
1295        page: int | None = None,
1296        page_size: int | None = None,
1297        ordering: str | None = None,
1298        descending: bool = False,
1299    ) -> list[CustomField]:
1300        """Return all custom fields defined in paperless-ngx.
1301
1302        Args:
1303            page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables
1304                auto-pagination. Default: ``None`` (return all).
1305            page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server
1306                default is used.
1307            ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: ``"name"``, ``"id"``.
1308            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
1309                ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
1310
1311        Returns:
1312            List of
1313            :class:`~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField`
1314            objects.
1315        """
1316        params: dict[str, Any] = {}
1317        if page is not None:
1318            params["page"] = page
1319        if page_size is not None:
1320            params["page_size"] = page_size
1321        if ordering is not None:
1322            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
1323        return await self._list_resource("custom_fields", CustomField, params or None)
1324
1325    async def get_custom_field(self, id: int) -> CustomField:
1326        """Fetch a single custom field by its ID.
1327
1328        Args:
1329            id: Numeric custom-field ID.
1330
1331        Returns:
1332            The :class:`~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField`
1333            with the given ID.
1334
1335        Raises:
1336            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no custom field
1337                exists with that ID.
1338        """
1339        return await self._get_resource("custom_fields", id, CustomField)
1340
1341    async def create_custom_field(
1342        self,
1343        *,
1344        name: str,
1345        data_type: str,
1346        extra_data: Any | None = None,
1347    ) -> CustomField:
1348        """Create a new custom field.
1349
1350        Args:
1351            name: Field name shown in the UI. Must be unique.
1352            data_type: Value type. One of ``"string"``, ``"boolean"``,
1353                ``"integer"``, ``"float"``, ``"monetary"``, ``"date"``,
1354                ``"url"``, ``"documentlink"``, ``"select"``.
1355            extra_data: Additional configuration for the field type. For
1356                ``data_type="select"``, pass
1357                ``{"select_options": ["Option A", "Option B"]}``. For all
1358                other types, leave as ``None``.
1359
1360        Returns:
1361            The newly created
1362            :class:`~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField`.
1363        """
1364        return await self._create_resource(
1365            "custom_fields",
1366            CustomField,
1367            name=name,
1368            data_type=data_type,
1369            extra_data=extra_data,
1370        )
1371
1372    async def update_custom_field(
1373        self,
1374        id: int,
1375        *,
1376        name: str | None = None,
1377        extra_data: Any | None = None,
1378    ) -> CustomField:
1379        """Partially update a custom field (PATCH semantics).
1380
1381        Note:
1382            ``data_type`` is intentionally excluded — the paperless-ngx API
1383            does not allow changing the type of an existing custom field. To
1384            change the type, delete and recreate the field.
1385
1386        Args:
1387            id: Numeric ID of the custom field to update.
1388            name: Field name shown in the UI. Must be unique.
1389            extra_data: Additional configuration for the field type (e.g.
1390                ``{"select_options": ["Option A", "Option B"]}`` for select
1391                fields). Passing ``None`` is a no-op and will not clear the
1392                existing value.
1393
1394        Returns:
1395            The updated
1396            :class:`~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField`.
1397        """
1398        return await self._update_resource(
1399            "custom_fields",
1400            id,
1401            CustomField,
1402            name=name,
1403            extra_data=extra_data,
1404        )
1405
1406    async def delete_custom_field(self, id: int) -> None:
1407        """Delete a custom field.
1408
1409        Args:
1410            id: Numeric ID of the custom field to delete.
1411
1412        Raises:
1413            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no custom field
1414                exists with that ID.
1415        """
1416        await self._delete_resource("custom_fields", id)

Async client for the paperless-ngx API.

All operations are flat methods on the client. String names are resolved to integer IDs automatically wherever the API requires IDs (tags, correspondents, document types, storage paths).

Use as an async context manager to ensure the underlying HTTP connection pool is closed when you are done:

Example: async with PaperlessClient(url="http://localhost:8000", api_key="abc") as client: docs = await client.list_documents()

PaperlessClient( url: str, api_key: str, *, poll_interval: float = 2.0, poll_timeout: float = 60.0) 
55    def __init__(
56        self,
57        url: str,
58        api_key: str,
59        *,
60        poll_interval: float = 2.0,
61        poll_timeout: float = 60.0,
62    ) -> None:
63        """Create an async paperless-ngx client.
64
65        Args:
66            url: Base URL of the paperless-ngx instance
67                (e.g. ``"http://localhost:8000"``).
68            api_key: API token.  Generate one in paperless-ngx under
69                *Settings → API → Generate Token*.
70            poll_interval: Seconds between status checks when ``wait=True``
71                is passed to :meth:`upload_document`.  Default: ``2.0``.
72            poll_timeout: Maximum seconds to wait for a document to finish
73                processing before raising
74                :exc:`~easypaperless.exceptions.TaskTimeoutError`.
75                Default: ``60.0``.
76        """
77        self._session = HttpSession(base_url=url, api_key=api_key)
78        self._resolver = NameResolver(self._session)
79        self._poll_interval = poll_interval
80        self._poll_timeout = poll_timeout

Create an async paperless-ngx client.

Args: url: Base URL of the paperless-ngx instance (e.g. "http://localhost:8000"). api_key: API token. Generate one in paperless-ngx under *Settings → API → Generate Token*. poll_interval: Seconds between status checks when wait=True is passed to upload_document(). Default: 2.0. poll_timeout: Maximum seconds to wait for a document to finish processing before raising ~easypaperless.exceptions.TaskTimeoutError. Default: 60.0.

async def close(self) -> None: 
82    async def close(self) -> None:
83        """Close the underlying HTTP connection pool.
84
85        Called automatically when used as an async context manager.
86        """
87        await self._session.close()

Close the underlying HTTP connection pool.

Called automatically when used as an async context manager.

async def get_document( self, id: int, *, include_metadata: bool = False) -> Document: 
 99    async def get_document(self, id: int, *, include_metadata: bool = False) -> Document:
100        """Fetch a single document by its ID.
101
102        Args:
103            id: Numeric paperless-ngx document ID.
104            include_metadata: When ``True``, the extended file-level metadata
105                (checksums, sizes, MIME type) is fetched concurrently from
106                ``/documents/{id}/metadata/`` and attached to
107                :attr:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document.metadata`.
108                Default: ``False``.
109
110        Returns:
111            The :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document` with the
112            given ID.  When ``include_metadata=True`` the ``metadata``
113            attribute is populated; otherwise it is ``None``.
114
115        Raises:
116            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists
117                with that ID.
118        """
119        if include_metadata:
120            doc_resp, meta_resp = await asyncio.gather(
121                self._session.get(f"/documents/{id}/"),
122                self._session.get(f"/documents/{id}/metadata/"),
123            )
124            data = doc_resp.json()
125            data["metadata"] = meta_resp.json()
126        else:
127            resp = await self._session.get(f"/documents/{id}/")
128            data = resp.json()
129        return Document.model_validate(data)

Fetch a single document by its ID.

Args: id: Numeric paperless-ngx document ID. include_metadata: When True, the extended file-level metadata (checksums, sizes, MIME type) is fetched concurrently from /documents/{id}/metadata/ and attached to ~easypaperless.models.documents.Document.metadata. Default: False.

Returns: The ~easypaperless.models.documents.Document with the given ID. When include_metadata=True the metadata attribute is populated; otherwise it is None.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists with that ID.

async def get_document_metadata(self, id: int) -> DocumentMetadata: 
131    async def get_document_metadata(self, id: int) -> DocumentMetadata:
132        """Fetch the extended file-level metadata for a document.
133
134        This is a lower-overhead alternative to
135        ``get_document(id, include_metadata=True)`` when you only need the
136        metadata and not the full document object.
137
138        Args:
139            id: Numeric paperless-ngx document ID.
140
141        Returns:
142            A :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.DocumentMetadata`
143            instance containing checksums, sizes, MIME type, and embedded
144            file metadata.
145
146        Raises:
147            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists
148                with that ID.
149        """
150        resp = await self._session.get(f"/documents/{id}/metadata/")
151        return DocumentMetadata.model_validate(resp.json())

Fetch the extended file-level metadata for a document.

This is a lower-overhead alternative to get_document(id, include_metadata=True) when you only need the metadata and not the full document object.

Args: id: Numeric paperless-ngx document ID.

Returns: A ~easypaperless.models.documents.DocumentMetadata instance containing checksums, sizes, MIME type, and embedded file metadata.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists with that ID.

async def list_documents( self, *, search: str | None = None, search_mode: str = 'title_or_text', tags: list[int | str] | None = None, any_tag: list[int | str] | None = None, exclude_tags: list[int | str] | None = None, correspondent: int | str | None = None, any_correspondent: list[int | str] | None = None, exclude_correspondents: list[int | str] | None = None, document_type: int | str | None = None, any_document_type: list[int | str] | None = None, exclude_document_types: list[int | str] | None = None, asn: int | None = None, created_after: str | None = None, created_before: str | None = None, added_after: str | None = None, added_before: str | None = None, modified_after: str | None = None, modified_before: str | None = None, checksum: str | None = None, page_size: int = 25, page: int | None = None, ordering: str | None = None, descending: bool = False, max_results: int | None = None) -> list[Document]: 
153    async def list_documents(
154        self,
155        *,
156        search: str | None = None,
157        search_mode: str = "title_or_text",
158        tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
159        any_tag: list[int | str] | None = None,
160        exclude_tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
161        correspondent: int | str | None = None,
162        any_correspondent: list[int | str] | None = None,
163        exclude_correspondents: list[int | str] | None = None,
164        document_type: int | str | None = None,
165        any_document_type: list[int | str] | None = None,
166        exclude_document_types: list[int | str] | None = None,
167        asn: int | None = None,
168        created_after: str | None = None,
169        created_before: str | None = None,
170        added_after: str | None = None,
171        added_before: str | None = None,
172        modified_after: str | None = None,
173        modified_before: str | None = None,
174        checksum: str | None = None,
175        page_size: int = 25,
176        page: int | None = None,
177        ordering: str | None = None,
178        descending: bool = False,
179        max_results: int | None = None,
180    ) -> list[Document]:
181        """Return a filtered list of documents.
182
183        All tag, correspondent, and document-type parameters accept either
184        integer IDs or string names — names are resolved to IDs transparently.
185
186        Args:
187            search: Search string.  Behaviour depends on ``search_mode``.
188            search_mode: How ``search`` is applied.  One of:
189
190                * ``"title_or_text"`` *(default)* — full-text search across
191                  title and OCR content (Whoosh FTS, raw API ``search``
192                  parameter).
193                * ``"title"`` — case-insensitive substring match on title
194                  only (raw API ``title__icontains``).
195                * ``"query"`` — paperless query language, e.g.
196                  ``"tag:invoice date:[2024 TO *]"`` (raw API ``query``).
197                * ``"original_filename"`` — case-insensitive substring match
198                  on the original file name (raw API
199                  ``original_filename__icontains``).
200
201            tags: Documents must have **all** of these tags (AND semantics).
202                Accepts tag IDs or tag names.
203            any_tag: Documents must have **at least one** of these tags
204                (OR semantics).  Accepts tag IDs or tag names.
205            exclude_tags: Documents must have **none** of these tags.
206                Accepts tag IDs or tag names.
207            correspondent: Filter to documents assigned to exactly this
208                correspondent.  Accepts a correspondent ID or name.
209            any_correspondent: Filter to documents assigned to **any** of
210                these correspondents (OR semantics).  Accepts IDs or names.
211                Takes precedence over ``correspondent`` when both are given.
212            exclude_correspondents: Exclude documents assigned to any of
213                these correspondents.  Accepts IDs or names.
214            document_type: Filter to documents of exactly this type.
215                Accepts a document-type ID or name.
216            any_document_type: Filter to documents whose type is **any** of
217                these (OR semantics).  Accepts IDs or names.  Takes
218                precedence over ``document_type`` when both are given.
219            exclude_document_types: Exclude documents whose type is any of
220                these.  Accepts IDs or names.
221            asn: Filter by archive serial number.
222            created_after: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
223                documents created **after** this date are returned.
224            created_before: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
225                documents created **before** this date are returned.
226            added_after: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
227                documents **added** (ingested) after this date are returned.
228            added_before: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
229                documents **added** (ingested) before this date are returned.
230            modified_after: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
231                documents **modified** after this date are returned.
232            modified_before: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
233                documents **modified** before this date are returned.
234            checksum: MD5 checksum of the original file (exact match).
235                Returns only the document whose original file matches this
236                checksum.
237            page_size: Number of results per API page.  Default: ``25``.
238                Increase to reduce the number of HTTP requests for large
239                result sets.
240            page: Return only this specific page of results (1-based). When
241                set, auto-pagination is disabled and only the results from
242                that single page are returned. Use together with ``page_size``
243                to control page size. Default: ``None`` (auto-paginate through
244                all pages).
245            ordering: Field name to sort by. Examples: ``"created"``,
246                ``"title"``, ``"added"``. Use together with ``descending`` to
247                control direction. Default: ``None`` (server default ordering).
248            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
249                ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
250                Default: ``False``.
251            max_results: Stop fetching pages once this many documents have
252                been collected and return at most this many results.
253                ``None`` *(default)* returns everything.
254
255        Returns:
256            List of :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`
257            objects.
258        """
259        params: dict[str, Any] = {"page_size": page_size}
260
261        if search is not None:
262            api_param = _SEARCH_MODE_MAP.get(search_mode, "search")
263            params[api_param] = search
264
265        if tags is not None:
266            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", tags)
267            params["tags__id__all"] = ",".join(str(t) for t in resolved)
268
269        if any_tag is not None:
270            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", any_tag)
271            params["tags__id__in"] = ",".join(str(t) for t in resolved)
272
273        if exclude_tags is not None:
274            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", exclude_tags)
275            params["tags__id__none"] = ",".join(str(t) for t in resolved)
276
277        if any_correspondent is not None:
278            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("correspondents", any_correspondent)
279            params["correspondent__id__in"] = ",".join(str(c) for c in resolved)
280        elif correspondent is not None:
281            resolved_id = await self._resolver.resolve("correspondents", correspondent)
282            params["correspondent__id__in"] = resolved_id
283
284        if exclude_correspondents is not None:
285            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("correspondents", exclude_correspondents)
286            params["correspondent__id__none"] = ",".join(str(c) for c in resolved)
287
288        if any_document_type is not None:
289            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("document_types", any_document_type)
290            params["document_type__id__in"] = ",".join(str(d) for d in resolved)
291        elif document_type is not None:
292            resolved_id = await self._resolver.resolve("document_types", document_type)
293            params["document_type"] = resolved_id
294
295        if exclude_document_types is not None:
296            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("document_types", exclude_document_types)
297            params["document_type__id__none"] = ",".join(str(d) for d in resolved)
298
299        if asn is not None:
300            params["archive_serial_number"] = asn
301
302        if created_after is not None:
303            params["created__date__gt"] = created_after
304
305        if created_before is not None:
306            params["created__date__lt"] = created_before
307
308        if added_after is not None:
309            params["added__date__gt"] = added_after
310
311        if added_before is not None:
312            params["added__date__lt"] = added_before
313
314        if modified_after is not None:
315            params["modified__date__gt"] = modified_after
316
317        if modified_before is not None:
318            params["modified__date__lt"] = modified_before
319
320        if checksum is not None:
321            params["checksum"] = checksum
322
323        if ordering is not None:
324            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
325
326        if page is not None:
327            params["page"] = page
328            resp = await self._session.get("/documents/", params=params)
329            items = resp.json().get("results", [])
330            if max_results is not None:
331                items = items[:max_results]
332            return [Document.model_validate(item) for item in items]
333
334        items = await self._session.get_all_pages("/documents/", params, max_results=max_results)
335        return [Document.model_validate(item) for item in items]

Return a filtered list of documents.

All tag, correspondent, and document-type parameters accept either integer IDs or string names — names are resolved to IDs transparently.

Args: search: Search string. Behaviour depends on search_mode. search_mode: How search is applied. One of:

    * ``"title_or_text"`` *(default)* — full-text search across
      title and OCR content (Whoosh FTS, raw API ``search``
      parameter).
    * ``"title"`` — case-insensitive substring match on title
      only (raw API ``title__icontains``).
    * ``"query"`` — paperless query language, e.g.
      ``"tag:invoice date:[2024 TO *]"`` (raw API ``query``).
    * ``"original_filename"`` — case-insensitive substring match
      on the original file name (raw API
      ``original_filename__icontains``).

tags: Documents must have **all** of these tags (AND semantics).
    Accepts tag IDs or tag names.
any_tag: Documents must have **at least one** of these tags
    (OR semantics).  Accepts tag IDs or tag names.
exclude_tags: Documents must have **none** of these tags.
    Accepts tag IDs or tag names.
correspondent: Filter to documents assigned to exactly this
