    correspondent.  Accepts a correspondent ID or name.
any_correspondent: Filter to documents assigned to **any** of
    these correspondents (OR semantics).  Accepts IDs or names.
    Takes precedence over ``correspondent`` when both are given.
exclude_correspondents: Exclude documents assigned to any of
    these correspondents.  Accepts IDs or names.
document_type: Filter to documents of exactly this type.
    Accepts a document-type ID or name.
any_document_type: Filter to documents whose type is **any** of
    these (OR semantics).  Accepts IDs or names.  Takes
    precedence over ``document_type`` when both are given.
exclude_document_types: Exclude documents whose type is any of
    these.  Accepts IDs or names.
asn: Filter by archive serial number.
created_after: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
    documents created **after** this date are returned.
created_before: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
    documents created **before** this date are returned.
added_after: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
    documents **added** (ingested) after this date are returned.
added_before: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
    documents **added** (ingested) before this date are returned.
modified_after: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
    documents **modified** after this date are returned.
modified_before: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
    documents **modified** before this date are returned.
checksum: MD5 checksum of the original file (exact match).
    Returns only the document whose original file matches this
    checksum.
page_size: Number of results per API page.  Default: ``25``.
    Increase to reduce the number of HTTP requests for large
    result sets.
page: Return only this specific page of results (1-based). When
    set, auto-pagination is disabled and only the results from
    that single page are returned. Use together with ``page_size``
    to control page size. Default: ``None`` (auto-paginate through
    all pages).
ordering: Field name to sort by. Examples: ``"created"``,
    ``"title"``, ``"added"``. Use together with ``descending`` to
    control direction. Default: ``None`` (server default ordering).
descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
    ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
    Default: ``False``.
max_results: Stop fetching pages once this many documents have
    been collected and return at most this many results.
    ``None`` *(default)* returns everything.

Returns: List of ~easypaperless.models.documents.Document objects.

async def update_document( self, id: int, *, title: str | None = None, date: str | None = None, correspondent: int | str | None = None, document_type: int | str | None = None, storage_path: int | str | None = None, tags: list[int | str] | None = None, asn: int | None = None, custom_fields: list[dict] | None = None) -> Document: 
337    async def update_document(
338        self,
339        id: int,
340        *,
341        title: str | None = None,
342        date: str | None = None,
343        correspondent: int | str | None = None,
344        document_type: int | str | None = None,
345        storage_path: int | str | None = None,
346        tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
347        asn: int | None = None,
348        custom_fields: list[dict] | None = None,
349    ) -> Document:
350        """Partially update a document (PATCH semantics — only passed fields change).
351
352        Args:
353            id: Numeric ID of the document to update.
354            title: New document title.
355            date: Creation date as an ISO-8601 string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).
356            correspondent: Correspondent to assign, as an ID or name.
357                Pass ``0`` to clear.
358            document_type: Document type to assign, as an ID or name.
359                Pass ``0`` to clear.
360            storage_path: Storage path to assign, as an ID or name.
361                Pass ``0`` to clear.
362            tags: Full replacement list of tags (IDs or names).  The existing
363                tag list is replaced, not merged.
364            asn: Archive serial number to assign.
365            custom_fields: List of ``{"field": <field_id>, "value": ...}``
366                dicts.  Replaces the existing custom-field values.
367
368        Returns:
369            The updated :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`.
370        """
371        logger.debug("Updating document id=%d", id)
372        payload: dict[str, Any] = {}
373
374        if title is not None:
375            payload["title"] = title
376        if date is not None:
377            payload["created"] = date
378        if correspondent is not None:
379            payload["correspondent"] = await self._resolver.resolve("correspondents", correspondent)
380        if document_type is not None:
381            payload["document_type"] = await self._resolver.resolve("document_types", document_type)
382        if storage_path is not None:
383            payload["storage_path"] = await self._resolver.resolve("storage_paths", storage_path)
384        if tags is not None:
385            payload["tags"] = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", tags)
386        if asn is not None:
387            payload["archive_serial_number"] = asn
388        if custom_fields is not None:
389            payload["custom_fields"] = custom_fields
390
391        resp = await self._session.patch(f"/documents/{id}/", json=payload)
392        return Document.model_validate(resp.json())

Partially update a document (PATCH semantics — only passed fields change).

Args: id: Numeric ID of the document to update. title: New document title. date: Creation date as an ISO-8601 string ("YYYY-MM-DD"). correspondent: Correspondent to assign, as an ID or name. Pass 0 to clear. document_type: Document type to assign, as an ID or name. Pass 0 to clear. storage_path: Storage path to assign, as an ID or name. Pass 0 to clear. tags: Full replacement list of tags (IDs or names). The existing tag list is replaced, not merged. asn: Archive serial number to assign. custom_fields: List of {"field": <field_id>, "value": ...} dicts. Replaces the existing custom-field values.

Returns: The updated ~easypaperless.models.documents.Document.

async def delete_document(self, id: int) -> None: 
394    async def delete_document(self, id: int) -> None:
395        """Permanently delete a document.
396
397        Args:
398            id: Numeric ID of the document to delete.
399
400        Raises:
401            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists
402                with that ID.
403        """
404        logger.debug("Deleting document id=%d", id)
405        await self._session.delete(f"/documents/{id}/")

Permanently delete a document.

Args: id: Numeric ID of the document to delete.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists with that ID.

async def download_document(self, id: int, *, original: bool = False) -> bytes: 
407    async def download_document(self, id: int, *, original: bool = False) -> bytes:
408        """Download the binary content of a document.
409
410        Args:
411            id: Numeric ID of the document to download.
412            original: If ``False`` *(default)*, returns the archived
413                (post-processed) PDF.  If ``True``, returns the original
414                file that was uploaded.
415
416        Returns:
417            Raw file bytes.
418        """
419        endpoint = "download" if original else "archive"
420        resp = await self._session.get_download(f"/documents/{id}/{endpoint}/")
421        content_type = resp.headers.get("content-type", "")
422        if "text/html" in content_type or resp.content[:9].lower().startswith(b"<!doctype"):
423            raise ServerError(
424                f"Download returned an HTML page (content-type: {content_type!r}). "
425                "The server redirected to a login page even after re-attaching auth. "
426                "Run with --verbose to see redirect details.",
427                status_code=None,
428            )
429        return resp.content

Download the binary content of a document.

Args: id: Numeric ID of the document to download. original: If False (default), returns the archived (post-processed) PDF. If True, returns the original file that was uploaded.

Returns: Raw file bytes.

async def upload_document( self, file: str | pathlib._local.Path, *, title: str | None = None, created: str | None = None, correspondent: int | str | None = None, document_type: int | str | None = None, storage_path: int | str | None = None, tags: list[int | str] | None = None, asn: int | None = None, wait: bool = False, poll_interval: float | None = None, poll_timeout: float | None = None) -> str | Document: 
435    async def upload_document(
436        self,
437        file: str | Path,
438        *,
439        title: str | None = None,
440        created: str | None = None,
441        correspondent: int | str | None = None,
442        document_type: int | str | None = None,
443        storage_path: int | str | None = None,
444        tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
445        asn: int | None = None,
446        wait: bool = False,
447        poll_interval: float | None = None,
448        poll_timeout: float | None = None,
449    ) -> str | Document:
450        """Upload a document to paperless-ngx.
451
452        Args:
453            file: Path to the file to upload (``str`` or
454                :class:`~pathlib.Path`).
455            title: Title to assign to the document.  Paperless-ngx derives
456                one from the file name if omitted.
457            created: Creation date as an ISO-8601 string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).
458            correspondent: Correspondent to assign, as an ID or name.
459            document_type: Document type to assign, as an ID or name.
460            storage_path: Storage path to assign, as an ID or name.
461            tags: Tags to assign, as IDs or names.
462            asn: Archive serial number to assign.
463            wait: If ``False`` *(default)*, returns immediately with the
464                task ID string.  If ``True``, polls the task-status endpoint
465                until processing completes and returns the resulting
466                :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`.
467            poll_interval: Override the client-level ``poll_interval`` for
468                this upload.  Only used when ``wait=True``.
469            poll_timeout: Override the client-level ``poll_timeout`` for
470                this upload.  Only used when ``wait=True``.
471
472        Returns:
473            The Celery task ID string when ``wait=False``, or the fully
474            processed :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`
475            when ``wait=True``.
476
477        Raises:
478            ~easypaperless.exceptions.UploadError: If paperless-ngx reports
479                that the processing task failed.
480            ~easypaperless.exceptions.TaskTimeoutError: If ``wait=True`` and
481                processing does not complete within the timeout.
482        """
483        file_path = Path(file)
484        file_bytes = file_path.read_bytes()
485        logger.info("Uploading %r (%d bytes)", file_path.name, len(file_bytes))
486
487        data: dict[str, Any] = {}
488        if title is not None:
489            data["title"] = title
490        if created is not None:
491            data["created"] = created
492        if correspondent is not None:
493            data["correspondent"] = await self._resolver.resolve("correspondents", correspondent)
494        if document_type is not None:
495            data["document_type"] = await self._resolver.resolve("document_types", document_type)
496        if storage_path is not None:
497            data["storage_path"] = await self._resolver.resolve("storage_paths", storage_path)
498        if tags is not None:
499            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", tags)
500            data["tags"] = resolved
501        if asn is not None:
502            data["archive_serial_number"] = asn
503
504        files = {"document": (file_path.name, file_bytes)}
505        resp = await self._session.post("/documents/post_document/", data=data, files=files)
506        task_id: str = resp.text.strip('"')
507        logger.debug("Upload accepted, task_id=%r", task_id)
508
509        if not wait:
510            return task_id
511
512        interval = poll_interval if poll_interval is not None else self._poll_interval
513        timeout = poll_timeout if poll_timeout is not None else self._poll_timeout
514        return await self._poll_task(task_id, poll_interval=interval, poll_timeout=timeout)

Upload a document to paperless-ngx.

Args: file: Path to the file to upload (str or ~pathlib.Path). title: Title to assign to the document. Paperless-ngx derives one from the file name if omitted. created: Creation date as an ISO-8601 string ("YYYY-MM-DD"). correspondent: Correspondent to assign, as an ID or name. document_type: Document type to assign, as an ID or name. storage_path: Storage path to assign, as an ID or name. tags: Tags to assign, as IDs or names. asn: Archive serial number to assign. wait: If False (default), returns immediately with the task ID string. If True, polls the task-status endpoint until processing completes and returns the resulting ~easypaperless.models.documents.Document. poll_interval: Override the client-level poll_interval for this upload. Only used when wait=True. poll_timeout: Override the client-level poll_timeout for this upload. Only used when wait=True.

Returns: The Celery task ID string when wait=False, or the fully processed ~easypaperless.models.documents.Document when wait=True.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.UploadError: If paperless-ngx reports that the processing task failed. ~easypaperless.exceptions.TaskTimeoutError: If wait=True and processing does not complete within the timeout.

async def bulk_edit(self, document_ids: list[int], method: str, **parameters: Any) -> None: 
548    async def bulk_edit(
549        self, document_ids: list[int], method: str, **parameters: Any
550    ) -> None:
551        """Execute a bulk-edit operation on a list of documents.
552
553        This is a low-level method; prefer the higher-level helpers such as
554        :meth:`bulk_add_tag`, :meth:`bulk_remove_tag`, and
555        :meth:`bulk_modify_tags`.
556
557        Args:
558            document_ids: List of document IDs to operate on.
559            method: Bulk-edit method name as recognised by paperless-ngx
560                (e.g. ``"add_tag"``, ``"remove_tag"``, ``"delete"``).
561            **parameters: Additional keyword arguments forwarded to the API as
562                the ``parameters`` object.
563        """
564        payload = {"documents": document_ids, "method": method, "parameters": parameters}
565        await self._session.post("/documents/bulk_edit/", json=payload)

Execute a bulk-edit operation on a list of documents.

This is a low-level method; prefer the higher-level helpers such as bulk_add_tag(), bulk_remove_tag(), and bulk_modify_tags().

Args: document_ids: List of document IDs to operate on. method: Bulk-edit method name as recognised by paperless-ngx (e.g. "add_tag", "remove_tag", "delete"). **parameters: Additional keyword arguments forwarded to the API as the parameters object.

async def bulk_add_tag(self, document_ids: list[int], tag: int | str) -> None: 
567    async def bulk_add_tag(self, document_ids: list[int], tag: int | str) -> None:
568        """Add a tag to multiple documents in a single request.
569
570        Args:
571            document_ids: List of document IDs to tag.
572            tag: Tag to add, as an ID or name.
573        """
574        tag_id = await self._resolver.resolve("tags", tag)
575        await self.bulk_edit(document_ids, "add_tag", tag=tag_id)

Add a tag to multiple documents in a single request.

Args: document_ids: List of document IDs to tag. tag: Tag to add, as an ID or name.

async def bulk_remove_tag(self, document_ids: list[int], tag: int | str) -> None: 
577    async def bulk_remove_tag(self, document_ids: list[int], tag: int | str) -> None:
578        """Remove a tag from multiple documents in a single request.
579
580        Args:
581            document_ids: List of document IDs to un-tag.
582            tag: Tag to remove, as an ID or name.
583        """
584        tag_id = await self._resolver.resolve("tags", tag)
585        await self.bulk_edit(document_ids, "remove_tag", tag=tag_id)

Remove a tag from multiple documents in a single request.

Args: document_ids: List of document IDs to un-tag. tag: Tag to remove, as an ID or name.

async def bulk_modify_tags( self, document_ids: list[int], *, add_tags: list[int | str] | None = None, remove_tags: list[int | str] | None = None) -> None: 
587    async def bulk_modify_tags(
588        self,
589        document_ids: list[int],
590        *,
591        add_tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
592        remove_tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
593    ) -> None:
594        """Add and/or remove tags on multiple documents atomically.
595
596        Args:
597            document_ids: List of document IDs to modify.
598            add_tags: Tags to add, as IDs or names.
599            remove_tags: Tags to remove, as IDs or names.
600        """
601        add_ids = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", add_tags or [])
602        remove_ids = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", remove_tags or [])
603        await self.bulk_edit(document_ids, "modify_tags", add_tags=add_ids, remove_tags=remove_ids)

Add and/or remove tags on multiple documents atomically.

Args: document_ids: List of document IDs to modify. add_tags: Tags to add, as IDs or names. remove_tags: Tags to remove, as IDs or names.

async def bulk_delete(self, document_ids: list[int]) -> None: 
605    async def bulk_delete(self, document_ids: list[int]) -> None:
606        """Permanently delete multiple documents in a single request.
607
608        Args:
609            document_ids: List of document IDs to delete.
610        """
611        await self.bulk_edit(document_ids, "delete")

Permanently delete multiple documents in a single request.

Args: document_ids: List of document IDs to delete.

async def bulk_edit_objects( self, object_type: str, object_ids: list[int], operation: str, **parameters: Any) -> None: 
613    async def bulk_edit_objects(
614        self,
615        object_type: str,
616        object_ids: list[int],
617        operation: str,
618        **parameters: Any,
619    ) -> None:
620        """Execute a bulk operation on non-document objects (tags, etc.).
621
622        Args:
623            object_type: The paperless-ngx object type string (e.g.
624                ``"tags"``, ``"correspondents"``).
625            object_ids: List of object IDs to operate on.
626            operation: Operation name recognised by the
627                ``/bulk_edit_objects/`` endpoint.
628            **parameters: Additional keyword arguments forwarded directly to
629                the API payload.
630        """
631        payload = {
632            "objects": object_ids,
633            "object_type": object_type,
634            "operation": operation,
635            **parameters,
636        }
637        await self._session.post("/bulk_edit_objects/", json=payload)

Execute a bulk operation on non-document objects (tags, etc.).

Args: object_type: The paperless-ngx object type string (e.g. "tags", "correspondents"). object_ids: List of object IDs to operate on. operation: Operation name recognised by the /bulk_edit_objects/ endpoint. **parameters: Additional keyword arguments forwarded directly to the API payload.

async def list_tags( self, *, ids: list[int] | None = None, name_contains: str | None = None, page: int | None = None, page_size: int | None = None, ordering: str | None = None, descending: bool = False) -> list[Tag]: 
683    async def list_tags(
684        self,
685        *,
686        ids: list[int] | None = None,
687        name_contains: str | None = None,
688        page: int | None = None,
689        page_size: int | None = None,
690        ordering: str | None = None,
691        descending: bool = False,
692    ) -> list[Tag]:
693        """Return tags defined in paperless-ngx.
694
695        Args:
696            ids: Return only tags whose ID is in this list.
697            name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on tag name
698                (raw API ``name__icontains``).
699            page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables
700                auto-pagination. Default: ``None`` (return all).
701            page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server
702                default is used.
703            ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: ``"name"``, ``"id"``.
704            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
705                ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
706
707        Returns:
708            List of :class:`~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag` objects.
709        """
710        params: dict[str, Any] = {}
711        if ids is not None:
712            params["id__in"] = ",".join(str(i) for i in ids)
713        if name_contains is not None:
714            params["name__icontains"] = name_contains
715        if page is not None:
716            params["page"] = page
717        if page_size is not None:
718            params["page_size"] = page_size
719        if ordering is not None:
720            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
721        return await self._list_resource("tags", Tag, params or None)

Return tags defined in paperless-ngx.

Args: ids: Return only tags whose ID is in this list. name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on tag name (raw API name__icontains). page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables auto-pagination. Default: None (return all). page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server default is used. ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: "name", "id". descending: When True, reverses the sort direction of ordering. Ignored when ordering is None.

Returns: List of ~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag objects.

async def get_tag(self, id: int) -> Tag: 
723    async def get_tag(self, id: int) -> Tag:
724        """Fetch a single tag by its ID.
725
726        Args:
727            id: Numeric tag ID.
728
729        Returns:
730            The :class:`~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag` with the given ID.
731
732        Raises:
733            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no tag exists with
734                that ID.
735        """
736        return await self._get_resource("tags", id, Tag)

Fetch a single tag by its ID.

Args: id: Numeric tag ID.

Returns: The ~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag with the given ID.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no tag exists with that ID.

async def create_tag( self, *, name: str, color: str | None = None, is_inbox_tag: bool | None = None, match: str | None = None, matching_algorithm: int | None = None, is_insensitive: bool | None = None, parent: int | None = None) -> Tag: 
738    async def create_tag(
739        self,
740        *,
741        name: str,
742        color: str | None = None,
743        is_inbox_tag: bool | None = None,
744        match: str | None = None,
745        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
746        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
747        parent: int | None = None,
748    ) -> Tag:
749        """Create a new tag.
750
751        Args:
752            name: Tag name. Must be unique.
753            color: Background colour in the paperless-ngx UI as a CSS hex
754                string (e.g. ``"#ff0000"``).
755            is_inbox_tag: When ``True``, newly ingested documents receive this
756                tag automatically until processed. At most one tag should be
757                the inbox tag.
758            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
759                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
760            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
761                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
762                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
763            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
764                case-insensitively.
765            parent: ID of an existing tag to use as parent, enabling
766                hierarchical tag trees. ``None`` creates a root-level tag.
767
768        Returns:
769            The newly created :class:`~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag`.
770        """
771        return await self._create_resource(
772            "tags",
773            Tag,
774            name=name,
775            color=color,
776            is_inbox_tag=is_inbox_tag,
777            match=match,
778            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
779            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
780            parent=parent,
781        )

Create a new tag.

Args: name: Tag name. Must be unique. color: Background colour in the paperless-ngx UI as a CSS hex string (e.g. "#ff0000"). is_inbox_tag: When True, newly ingested documents receive this tag automatically until processed. At most one tag should be the inbox tag. match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document content. Interpretation depends on matching_algorithm. matching_algorithm: Controls how match is applied: 0=none, 1=any word, 2=all words, 3=exact, 4=regex, 5=fuzzy, 6=auto (ML). is_insensitive: When True, match is evaluated case-insensitively. parent: ID of an existing tag to use as parent, enabling hierarchical tag trees. None creates a root-level tag.

Returns: The newly created ~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag.

async def update_tag( self, id: int, *, name: str | None = None, color: str | None = None, is_inbox_tag: bool | None = None, match: str | None = None, matching_algorithm: int | None = None, is_insensitive: bool | None = None, parent: int | None = None) -> Tag: 
783    async def update_tag(
784        self,
785        id: int,
786        *,
787        name: str | None = None,
788        color: str | None = None,
789        is_inbox_tag: bool | None = None,
790        match: str | None = None,
791        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
792        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
793        parent: int | None = None,
794    ) -> Tag:
795        """Partially update a tag (PATCH semantics).
796
797        Args:
798            id: Numeric ID of the tag to update.
799            name: Tag name. Must be unique.
800            color: Background colour in the paperless-ngx UI as a CSS hex
801                string (e.g. ``"#00ff00"``).
802            is_inbox_tag: When ``True``, newly ingested documents receive this
803                tag automatically until processed. At most one tag should be
804                the inbox tag.
805            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
806                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
807            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
808                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
809                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
810            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
811                case-insensitively.
812            parent: ID of an existing tag to use as parent, enabling
813                hierarchical tag trees.
814
815        Returns:
816            The updated :class:`~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag`.
817        """
818        return await self._update_resource(
819            "tags",
820            id,
821            Tag,
822            name=name,
823            color=color,
824            is_inbox_tag=is_inbox_tag,
825            match=match,
826            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
827            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
828            parent=parent,
829        )

Partially update a tag (PATCH semantics).

Args: id: Numeric ID of the tag to update. name: Tag name. Must be unique. color: Background colour in the paperless-ngx UI as a CSS hex string (e.g. "#00ff00"). is_inbox_tag: When True, newly ingested documents receive this tag automatically until processed. At most one tag should be the inbox tag. match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document content. Interpretation depends on matching_algorithm. matching_algorithm: Controls how match is applied: 0=none, 1=any word, 2=all words, 3=exact, 4=regex, 5=fuzzy, 6=auto (ML). is_insensitive: When True, match is evaluated case-insensitively. parent: ID of an existing tag to use as parent, enabling hierarchical tag trees.

Returns: The updated ~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag.

async def delete_tag(self, id: int) -> None: 
831    async def delete_tag(self, id: int) -> None:
832        """Delete a tag.
833
834        Args:
835            id: Numeric ID of the tag to delete.
836
837        Raises:
838            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no tag exists with
839                that ID.
840        """
841        await self._delete_resource("tags", id)

Delete a tag.

Args: id: Numeric ID of the tag to delete.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no tag exists with that ID.

async def list_correspondents( self, *, ids: list[int] | None = None, name_contains: str | None = None, page: int | None = None, page_size: int | None = None, ordering: str | None = None, descending: bool = False) -> list[Correspondent]: 
847    async def list_correspondents(
848        self,
849        *,
850        ids: list[int] | None = None,
851        name_contains: str | None = None,
852        page: int | None = None,
853        page_size: int | None = None,
854        ordering: str | None = None,
855        descending: bool = False,
856    ) -> list[Correspondent]:
857        """Return correspondents defined in paperless-ngx.
858
859        Args:
860            ids: Return only correspondents whose ID is in this list.
861            name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on correspondent
862                name (raw API ``name__icontains``).
863            page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables
864                auto-pagination. Default: ``None`` (return all).
865            page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server
866                default is used.
867            ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: ``"name"``, ``"id"``.
868            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
869                ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
870
871        Returns:
872            List of
873            :class:`~easypaperless.models.correspondents.Correspondent`
874            objects.
875        """
876        params: dict[str, Any] = {}
877        if ids is not None:
878            params["id__in"] = ",".join(str(i) for i in ids)
879        if name_contains is not None:
880            params["name__icontains"] = name_contains
881        if page is not None:
882            params["page"] = page
883        if page_size is not None:
884            params["page_size"] = page_size
885        if ordering is not None:
886            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
887        return await self._list_resource("correspondents", Correspondent, params or None)

Return correspondents defined in paperless-ngx.

Args: ids: Return only correspondents whose ID is in this list. name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on correspondent name (raw API name__icontains). page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables auto-pagination. Default: None (return all). page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server default is used. ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: "name", "id". descending: When True, reverses the sort direction of ordering. Ignored when ordering is None.

Returns: List of ~easypaperless.models.correspondents.Correspondent objects.

async def get_correspondent(self, id: int) -> Correspondent: 
889    async def get_correspondent(self, id: int) -> Correspondent:
890        """Fetch a single correspondent by its ID.
891
892        Args:
893            id: Numeric correspondent ID.
894
895        Returns:
896            The :class:`~easypaperless.models.correspondents.Correspondent`
897            with the given ID.
898
899        Raises:
900            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no correspondent
901                exists with that ID.
902        """
903        return await self._get_resource("correspondents", id, Correspondent)

Fetch a single correspondent by its ID.

Args: id: Numeric correspondent ID.

Returns: The ~easypaperless.models.correspondents.Correspondent with the given ID.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no correspondent exists with that ID.

async def create_correspondent( self, *, name: str, match: str | None = None, matching_algorithm: int | None = None, is_insensitive: bool | None = None) -> Correspondent: 
905    async def create_correspondent(
906        self,
907        *,
908        name: str,
909        match: str | None = None,
910        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
911        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
912    ) -> Correspondent:
913        """Create a new correspondent.
914
915        Args:
916            name: Correspondent name (sender/recipient). Must be unique.
917            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
918                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
919            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
920                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
921                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
922            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
923                case-insensitively.
924
925        Returns:
926            The newly created
927            :class:`~easypaperless.models.correspondents.Correspondent`.
928        """
929        return await self._create_resource(
930            "correspondents",
931            Correspondent,
932            name=name,
933            match=match,
934            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
935            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
936        )

Create a new correspondent.

Args: name: Correspondent name (sender/recipient). Must be unique. match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document content. Interpretation depends on matching_algorithm. matching_algorithm: Controls how match is applied: 0=none, 1=any word, 2=all words, 3=exact, 4=regex, 5=fuzzy, 6=auto (ML). is_insensitive: When True, match is evaluated case-insensitively.

Returns: The newly created ~easypaperless.models.correspondents.Correspondent.

954            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
955                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,

968            name=name,

970            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
971            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
972        )

1008            page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server

1028        if ordering is not None:

1030        return await self._list_resource("document_types", DocumentType, params or None)

1135    async def list_storage_paths(
1136        self,
1137        *,
1138        ids: list[int] | None = None,
1139        name_contains: str | None = None,
1140        page: int | None = None,

1142        ordering: str | None = None,
1143        descending: bool = False,
1144    ) -> list[StoragePath]:
1145        """Return storage paths defined in paperless-ngx.
1146
1147        Args:
1148            ids: Return only storage paths whose ID is in this list.
1149            name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on storage-path
1150                name (raw API ``name__icontains``).
1151            page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables
1152                auto-pagination. Default: ``None`` (return all).
1153            page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server
1154                default is used.
1155            ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: ``"name"``, ``"id"``.
1156            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
1157                ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
1158
1159        Returns:
1160            List of
1161            :class:`~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath`
1162            objects.

1165        if ids is not None:

1167        if name_contains is not None:
1168            params["name__icontains"] = name_contains
1169        if page is not None:
1170            params["page"] = page
1171        if page_size is not None:
1172            params["page_size"] = page_size
1173        if ordering is not None:
1174            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
1175        return await self._list_resource("storage_paths", StoragePath, params or None)
