async def create_storage_path( self, *, name: str, path: str | None = None, match: str | None = None, matching_algorithm: int | None = None, is_insensitive: bool | None = None) -> StoragePath: 
1193    async def create_storage_path(
1194        self,
1195        *,
1196        name: str,
1197        path: str | None = None,
1198        match: str | None = None,
1199        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
1200        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
1201    ) -> StoragePath:
1202        """Create a new storage path.
1203
1204        Args:
1205            name: Storage-path name. Must be unique.
1206            path: Template string for the archive file path. Supported
1207                placeholders: ``{created_year}``, ``{created_month}``,
1208                ``{created_day}``, ``{correspondent}``, ``{document_type}``,
1209                ``{title}``, ``{asn}``. Example:
1210                ``"{created_year}/{correspondent}/{title}"``. When omitted,
1211                the server default location is used.
1212            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
1213                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
1214            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
1215                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
1216                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
1217            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
1218                case-insensitively.
1219
1220        Returns:
1221            The newly created
1222            :class:`~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath`.
1223        """
1224        return await self._create_resource(
1225            "storage_paths",
1226            StoragePath,
1227            name=name,
1228            path=path,
1229            match=match,
1230            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
1231            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
1232        )

Create a new storage path.

Args: name: Storage-path name. Must be unique. path: Template string for the archive file path. Supported placeholders: {created_year}, {created_month}, {created_day}, {correspondent}, {document_type}, {title}, {asn}. Example: "{created_year}/{correspondent}/{title}". When omitted, the server default location is used. match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document content. Interpretation depends on matching_algorithm. matching_algorithm: Controls how match is applied: 0=none, 1=any word, 2=all words, 3=exact, 4=regex, 5=fuzzy, 6=auto (ML). is_insensitive: When True, match is evaluated case-insensitively.

Returns: The newly created ~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath.

async def update_storage_path( self, id: int, *, name: str | None = None, path: str | None = None, match: str | None = None, matching_algorithm: int | None = None, is_insensitive: bool | None = None) -> StoragePath: 
1234    async def update_storage_path(
1235        self,
1236        id: int,
1237        *,
1238        name: str | None = None,
1239        path: str | None = None,
1240        match: str | None = None,
1241        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
1242        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
1243    ) -> StoragePath:
1244        """Partially update a storage path (PATCH semantics).
1245
1246        Args:
1247            id: Numeric ID of the storage path to update.
1248            name: Storage-path name. Must be unique.
1249            path: Template string for the archive file path. Supported
1250                placeholders: ``{created_year}``, ``{created_month}``,
1251                ``{created_day}``, ``{correspondent}``, ``{document_type}``,
1252                ``{title}``, ``{asn}``. Example: ``"{title}"``.
1253            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
1254                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
1255            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
1256                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
1257                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
1258            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
1259                case-insensitively.
1260
1261        Returns:
1262            The updated
1263            :class:`~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath`.
1264        """
1265        return await self._update_resource(
1266            "storage_paths",
1267            id,
1268            StoragePath,
1269            name=name,
1270            path=path,
1271            match=match,
1272            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
1273            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
1274        )

Partially update a storage path (PATCH semantics).

Args: id: Numeric ID of the storage path to update. name: Storage-path name. Must be unique. path: Template string for the archive file path. Supported placeholders: {created_year}, {created_month}, {created_day}, {correspondent}, {document_type}, {title}, {asn}. Example: "{title}". match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document content. Interpretation depends on matching_algorithm. matching_algorithm: Controls how match is applied: 0=none, 1=any word, 2=all words, 3=exact, 4=regex, 5=fuzzy, 6=auto (ML). is_insensitive: When True, match is evaluated case-insensitively.

Returns: The updated ~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath.

async def delete_storage_path(self, id: int) -> None: 
1276    async def delete_storage_path(self, id: int) -> None:
1277        """Delete a storage path.
1278
1279        Args:
1280            id: Numeric ID of the storage path to delete.
1281
1282        Raises:
1283            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no storage path
1284                exists with that ID.
1285        """
1286        await self._delete_resource("storage_paths", id)

Delete a storage path.

Args: id: Numeric ID of the storage path to delete.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no storage path exists with that ID.

async def list_custom_fields( self, *, page: int | None = None, page_size: int | None = None, ordering: str | None = None, descending: bool = False) -> list[CustomField]: 
1292    async def list_custom_fields(
1293        self,
1294        *,
1295        page: int | None = None,
1296        page_size: int | None = None,
1297        ordering: str | None = None,
1298        descending: bool = False,
1299    ) -> list[CustomField]:
1300        """Return all custom fields defined in paperless-ngx.
1301
1302        Args:
1303            page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables
1304                auto-pagination. Default: ``None`` (return all).
1305            page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server
1306                default is used.
1307            ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: ``"name"``, ``"id"``.
1308            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
1309                ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
1310
1311        Returns:
1312            List of
1313            :class:`~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField`
1314            objects.
1315        """
1316        params: dict[str, Any] = {}
1317        if page is not None:
1318            params["page"] = page
1319        if page_size is not None:
1320            params["page_size"] = page_size
1321        if ordering is not None:
1322            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
1323        return await self._list_resource("custom_fields", CustomField, params or None)

Return all custom fields defined in paperless-ngx.

Args: page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables auto-pagination. Default: None (return all). page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server default is used. ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: "name", "id". descending: When True, reverses the sort direction of ordering. Ignored when ordering is None.

Returns: List of ~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField objects.

async def get_custom_field(self, id: int) -> CustomField: 
1325    async def get_custom_field(self, id: int) -> CustomField:
1326        """Fetch a single custom field by its ID.
1327
1328        Args:
1329            id: Numeric custom-field ID.
1330
1331        Returns:
1332            The :class:`~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField`
1333            with the given ID.
1334
1335        Raises:
1336            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no custom field
1337                exists with that ID.
1338        """
1339        return await self._get_resource("custom_fields", id, CustomField)

Fetch a single custom field by its ID.

Args: id: Numeric custom-field ID.

Returns: The ~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField with the given ID.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no custom field exists with that ID.

async def create_custom_field( self, *, name: str, data_type: str, extra_data: typing.Any | None = None) -> CustomField: 
1341    async def create_custom_field(
1342        self,
1343        *,
1344        name: str,
1345        data_type: str,
1346        extra_data: Any | None = None,
1347    ) -> CustomField:
1348        """Create a new custom field.
1349
1350        Args:
1351            name: Field name shown in the UI. Must be unique.
1352            data_type: Value type. One of ``"string"``, ``"boolean"``,
1353                ``"integer"``, ``"float"``, ``"monetary"``, ``"date"``,
1354                ``"url"``, ``"documentlink"``, ``"select"``.
1355            extra_data: Additional configuration for the field type. For
1356                ``data_type="select"``, pass
1357                ``{"select_options": ["Option A", "Option B"]}``. For all
1358                other types, leave as ``None``.
1359
1360        Returns:
1361            The newly created
1362            :class:`~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField`.
1363        """
1364        return await self._create_resource(
1365            "custom_fields",
1366            CustomField,
1367            name=name,
1368            data_type=data_type,
1369            extra_data=extra_data,
1370        )

Create a new custom field.

Args: name: Field name shown in the UI. Must be unique. data_type: Value type. One of "string", "boolean", "integer", "float", "monetary", "date", "url", "documentlink", "select". extra_data: Additional configuration for the field type. For data_type="select", pass {"select_options": ["Option A", "Option B"]}. For all other types, leave as None.

Returns: The newly created ~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField.

async def update_custom_field( self, id: int, *, name: str | None = None, extra_data: typing.Any | None = None) -> CustomField: 
1372    async def update_custom_field(
1373        self,
1374        id: int,
1375        *,
1376        name: str | None = None,
1377        extra_data: Any | None = None,
1378    ) -> CustomField:
1379        """Partially update a custom field (PATCH semantics).
1380
1381        Note:
1382            ``data_type`` is intentionally excluded — the paperless-ngx API
1383            does not allow changing the type of an existing custom field. To
1384            change the type, delete and recreate the field.
1385
1386        Args:
1387            id: Numeric ID of the custom field to update.
1388            name: Field name shown in the UI. Must be unique.
1389            extra_data: Additional configuration for the field type (e.g.
1390                ``{"select_options": ["Option A", "Option B"]}`` for select
1391                fields). Passing ``None`` is a no-op and will not clear the
1392                existing value.
1393
1394        Returns:
1395            The updated
1396            :class:`~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField`.
1397        """
1398        return await self._update_resource(
1399            "custom_fields",
1400            id,
1401            CustomField,
1402            name=name,
1403            extra_data=extra_data,
1404        )

Partially update a custom field (PATCH semantics).

Note: data_type is intentionally excluded — the paperless-ngx API does not allow changing the type of an existing custom field. To change the type, delete and recreate the field.

Args: id: Numeric ID of the custom field to update. name: Field name shown in the UI. Must be unique. extra_data: Additional configuration for the field type (e.g. {"select_options": ["Option A", "Option B"]} for select fields). Passing None is a no-op and will not clear the existing value.

Returns: The updated ~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField.

async def delete_custom_field(self, id: int) -> None: 
1406    async def delete_custom_field(self, id: int) -> None:
1407        """Delete a custom field.
1408
1409        Args:
1410            id: Numeric ID of the custom field to delete.
1411
1412        Raises:
1413            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no custom field
1414                exists with that ID.
1415        """
1416        await self._delete_resource("custom_fields", id)

Delete a custom field.

Args: id: Numeric ID of the custom field to delete.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no custom field exists with that ID.

class SyncPaperlessClient: 
18class SyncPaperlessClient:
19    """Synchronous interface to paperless-ngx.
20
21    Exposes the same methods as
22    :class:`~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient` but runs them
23    synchronously, making it suitable for scripts and REPL sessions that do
24    not use ``asyncio``.
25
26    All async methods of ``PaperlessClient`` are available here with the same
27    signatures.  Operations run on a dedicated background event loop thread so
28    that the httpx connection pool is reused across calls and cleanup works
29    correctly — unlike ``asyncio.run()``, which creates and destroys a loop
30    per call and leaves transports in a broken state on subsequent calls.
31
32    Use as a context manager to ensure proper cleanup:
33
34    Example:
35        with SyncPaperlessClient(url="http://localhost:8000", api_key="abc") as client:
36            tags = client.list_tags()
37            docs = client.list_documents(search="invoice")
38
39    Note:
40        Cannot be used inside an already-running event loop (e.g. Jupyter
41        notebooks).  Use :class:`~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient`
42        directly in those environments.
43
44    No business logic lives here — this class is a pure thin wrapper.
45    """
46
47    def __init__(self, url: str, api_key: str, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
48        """Create a synchronous paperless-ngx client.
49
50        Args:
51            url: Base URL of the paperless-ngx instance
52                (e.g. ``"http://localhost:8000"``).
53            api_key: API token.  Generate one in paperless-ngx under
54                *Settings → API → Generate Token*.
55            **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments forwarded to
56                :class:`~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient` (e.g.
57                ``poll_interval``, ``poll_timeout``).
58        """
59        self._loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()
60        self._thread = threading.Thread(target=self._loop.run_forever, daemon=True)
61        self._thread.start()
62        self._async_client = PaperlessClient(url, api_key, **kwargs)
63
64    def _run(self, coro: Any) -> Any:
65        """Submit a coroutine to the background event loop and block until done."""
66        future = asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe(coro, self._loop)
67        return future.result()
68
69    def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> Any:
70        attr = getattr(self._async_client, name)
71        if inspect.iscoroutinefunction(attr):
72            def wrapper(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> Any:
73                return self._run(attr(*args, **kwargs))
74            wrapper.__name__ = name
75            return wrapper
76        return attr
77
78    def close(self) -> None:
79        """Close the underlying HTTP connection pool and stop the event loop.
80
81        Called automatically when used as a context manager.
82        """
83        self._run(self._async_client.close())
84        self._loop.call_soon_threadsafe(self._loop.stop)
85        self._thread.join()
86        self._loop.close()
87
88    def __enter__(self) -> SyncPaperlessClient:
89        return self
90
91    def __exit__(self, *args: Any) -> None:
92        self.close()

Synchronous interface to paperless-ngx.

Exposes the same methods as ~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient but runs them synchronously, making it suitable for scripts and REPL sessions that do not use asyncio.

All async methods of PaperlessClient are available here with the same signatures. Operations run on a dedicated background event loop thread so that the httpx connection pool is reused across calls and cleanup works correctly — unlike asyncio.run(), which creates and destroys a loop per call and leaves transports in a broken state on subsequent calls.

Use as a context manager to ensure proper cleanup:

Example: with SyncPaperlessClient(url="http://localhost:8000", api_key="abc") as client: tags = client.list_tags() docs = client.list_documents(search="invoice")

Note: Cannot be used inside an already-running event loop (e.g. Jupyter notebooks). Use ~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient directly in those environments.

No business logic lives here — this class is a pure thin wrapper.

SyncPaperlessClient(url: str, api_key: str, **kwargs: Any) 
47    def __init__(self, url: str, api_key: str, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
48        """Create a synchronous paperless-ngx client.
49
50        Args:
51            url: Base URL of the paperless-ngx instance
52                (e.g. ``"http://localhost:8000"``).
53            api_key: API token.  Generate one in paperless-ngx under
54                *Settings → API → Generate Token*.
55            **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments forwarded to
56                :class:`~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient` (e.g.
57                ``poll_interval``, ``poll_timeout``).
58        """
59        self._loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()
60        self._thread = threading.Thread(target=self._loop.run_forever, daemon=True)
61        self._thread.start()
62        self._async_client = PaperlessClient(url, api_key, **kwargs)

Create a synchronous paperless-ngx client.

Args: url: Base URL of the paperless-ngx instance (e.g. "http://localhost:8000"). api_key: API token. Generate one in paperless-ngx under Settings → API → Generate Token. **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments forwarded to ~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient (e.g. poll_interval, poll_timeout).

def close(self) -> None: 
78    def close(self) -> None:
79        """Close the underlying HTTP connection pool and stop the event loop.
80
81        Called automatically when used as a context manager.
82        """
83        self._run(self._async_client.close())
84        self._loop.call_soon_threadsafe(self._loop.stop)
85        self._thread.join()
86        self._loop.close()

Close the underlying HTTP connection pool and stop the event loop.

Called automatically when used as a context manager.

class DocumentStore: 
 68class DocumentStore:
 69    """SQLite-backed local mirror of paperless-ngx document metadata.
 70
 71    Wraps a :class:`~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient` and mirrors
 72    document metadata (plus tags, correspondents, document types, and storage
 73    paths) into a local SQLite database for fast, offline-capable search.
 74
 75    Sync is **manual and explicit**: call :meth:`sync` to pull fresh data from
 76    the server.  :meth:`search_documents` is a pure SQLite query — no network
 77    is involved.
 78
 79    Example:
 80        async with PaperlessClient(url="...", api_key="...") as client:
 81            store = DocumentStore(client, "paperless.db")
 82            await store.sync()
 83            results = store.search_documents(tags=["invoice"], created_after="2024-01-01")
 84            store.close()
 85    """
 86
 87    def __init__(self, client: PaperlessClient, db_path: str | Path) -> None:
 88        """Create a DocumentStore backed by a local SQLite file.
 89
 90        Args:
 91            client: An initialised
 92                :class:`~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient` used for
 93                fetching data during :meth:`sync`.
 94            db_path: Path to the SQLite database file.  The file is created
 95                if it does not exist.
 96        """
 97        self._client = client
 98        self._db_path = Path(db_path)
 99        self._conn: sqlite3.Connection | None = None
100
101    def _get_conn(self) -> sqlite3.Connection:
102        if self._conn is None:
103            logger.debug("Opening SQLite database at %s", self._db_path)
104            self._conn = sqlite3.connect(self._db_path)
105            self._conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
106            self._conn.executescript(_DDL)
107            self._conn.commit()
108        return self._conn
109
110    def close(self) -> None:
111        """Close the SQLite database connection."""
112        if self._conn is not None:
113            self._conn.close()
114            self._conn = None
115
116    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
117    # Sync
118    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
119
120    async def sync(self) -> int:
121        """Pull all documents and supporting metadata from the server and upsert locally.
122
123        Fetches documents, tags, correspondents, document types, and storage
124        paths in parallel, then upserts everything into SQLite.  Existing
125        local records are replaced; documents no longer present on the server
126        are *not* removed (incremental sync is not yet implemented).
127
128        Returns:
129            Number of documents synced.
130        """
131        logger.info("Starting sync from server")
132        docs, tags, correspondents, doc_types, storage_paths = await asyncio.gather(
133            self._client.list_documents(),
134            self._client.list_tags(),
135            self._client.list_correspondents(),
136            self._client.list_document_types(),
137            self._client.list_storage_paths(),
138        )
139
140        conn = self._get_conn()
141
142        # Upsert tags
143        conn.executemany(
144            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO tags (id, name, raw_json) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
145            [(t.id, t.name, t.model_dump_json()) for t in tags],
146        )
147
148        # Upsert correspondents
149        conn.executemany(
150            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO correspondents (id, name, raw_json) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
151            [(c.id, c.name, c.model_dump_json()) for c in correspondents],
152        )
153
154        # Upsert document types
155        conn.executemany(
156            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO document_types (id, name, raw_json) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
157            [(dt.id, dt.name, dt.model_dump_json()) for dt in doc_types],
158        )
159
160        # Upsert storage paths
161        conn.executemany(
162            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO storage_paths (id, name, raw_json) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
163            [(sp.id, sp.name, sp.model_dump_json()) for sp in storage_paths],
164        )
165
166        # Upsert documents
167        for doc in docs:
168            created_date_str = doc.created_date.isoformat() if doc.created_date else None
169            conn.execute(
170                """INSERT OR REPLACE INTO documents
171                   (id, title, correspondent_id, document_type_id, created_date, raw_json)
172                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
173                (
174                    doc.id,
175                    doc.title,
176                    doc.correspondent,
177                    doc.document_type,
178                    created_date_str,
179                    doc.model_dump_json(),
180                ),
181            )
182            # Rebuild document_tags
183            conn.execute("DELETE FROM document_tags WHERE document_id = ?", (doc.id,))
184            conn.executemany(
185                "INSERT OR IGNORE INTO document_tags (document_id, tag_id) VALUES (?, ?)",
186                [(doc.id, tag_id) for tag_id in doc.tags],
187            )
188
189        from datetime import datetime, timezone
190        conn.execute(
191            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO sync_metadata (key, value) VALUES ('last_sync', ?)",
192            (datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),),
193        )
194        conn.commit()
195        logger.info(
196            "Sync complete: %d documents, %d tags, %d correspondents, %d document_types, %d storage_paths",
197            len(docs), len(tags), len(correspondents), len(doc_types), len(storage_paths),
198        )
199
200        return len(docs)
201
202    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
203    # Search (pure SQLite, no network)
204    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
205
206    def search_documents(
207        self,
208        *,
209        title_contains: str | None = None,
210        title_regex: str | None = None,
211        content_regex: str | None = None,
212        tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
213        created_after: str | None = None,
214        created_before: str | None = None,
215        correspondent: int | str | None = None,
216    ) -> list[Document]:
217        """Search the local SQLite cache — no network request is made.
218
219        SQL filters (``title_contains``, ``created_after``, ``created_before``,
220        ``correspondent``, ``tags``) are applied first.  Regex filters
221        (``title_regex``, ``content_regex``) are then applied in Python on the
222        resulting rows.
223
224        Args:
225            title_contains: Case-insensitive substring match on the document
226                title.  Applied as a SQL ``LIKE`` filter.
227            title_regex: Python ``re`` pattern applied to the document title
228                (case-insensitive).  Applied after SQL filtering.
229            content_regex: Python ``re`` pattern applied to the OCR content of
230                each candidate document (case-insensitive).  Applied after SQL
231                filtering.  Content must have been present when :meth:`sync`
232                was last called.
233            tags: Documents must have **all** of these tags.  Accepts tag IDs
234                or tag names (resolved from the local cache).
235            created_after: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
236                documents created **after** this date are returned.
237            created_before: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
238                documents created **before** this date are returned.
239            correspondent: Filter to documents assigned to this correspondent.
240                Accepts a correspondent ID or name (resolved from the local
241                cache).
242
243        Returns:
244            List of :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`
245            objects matching all supplied filters.
246
247        Raises:
248            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If a tag or correspondent
249                name is supplied that does not exist in the local cache.
250        """
251        active_filters = {
252            k: v for k, v in {
253                "title_contains": title_contains,
254                "title_regex": title_regex,
255                "content_regex": content_regex,
256                "tags": tags,
257                "created_after": created_after,
258                "created_before": created_before,
259                "correspondent": correspondent,
260            }.items() if v is not None
261        }
262        if active_filters:
263            logger.debug(
264                "search_documents(%s)",
265                ", ".join(f"{k}={v!r}" for k, v in active_filters.items()),
266            )
267        else:
268            logger.debug("search_documents()")
269
270        conn = self._get_conn()
271
272        conditions: list[str] = []
273        bind: list[Any] = []
274
275        if title_contains is not None:
276            conditions.append("d.title LIKE ?")
277            bind.append(f"%{title_contains}%")
278
279        if created_after is not None:
280            conditions.append("d.created_date > ?")
281            bind.append(created_after)
282
283        if created_before is not None:
284            conditions.append("d.created_date < ?")
285            bind.append(created_before)
286
287        if correspondent is not None:
288            corr_id = self._resolve_local("correspondents", correspondent)
289            conditions.append("d.correspondent_id = ?")
290            bind.append(corr_id)
291
292        if tags:
293            tag_ids = [self._resolve_local("tags", t) for t in tags]
294            for tag_id in tag_ids:
295                conditions.append(
296                    "EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM document_tags dt WHERE dt.document_id = d.id AND dt.tag_id = ?)"
297                )
298                bind.append(tag_id)
299
300        where_clause = ("WHERE " + " AND ".join(conditions)) if conditions else ""
301        sql = f"SELECT d.raw_json FROM documents d {where_clause}"
302        rows = conn.execute(sql, bind).fetchall()
303
304        results: list[Document] = []
305        title_re = re.compile(title_regex, re.IGNORECASE) if title_regex else None
306        content_re = re.compile(content_regex, re.IGNORECASE) if content_regex else None
307
308        for row in rows:
309            data = json.loads(row["raw_json"])
310            if title_re and not title_re.search(data.get("title", "")):
311                continue
312            if content_re and not content_re.search(data.get("content", "") or ""):
313                continue
314            results.append(Document.model_validate(data))
315
316        logger.debug("search_documents returned %d result(s)", len(results))
317        return results
318
319    def _resolve_local(self, table: str, value: int | str) -> int:
320        if isinstance(value, int):
321            return value
322        conn = self._get_conn()
323        row = conn.execute(
324            f"SELECT id FROM {table} WHERE LOWER(name) = ?", (value.lower(),)
325        ).fetchone()
326        if row is None:
327            from easypaperless.exceptions import NotFoundError
328            raise NotFoundError(f"{table!r} item with name {value!r} not found in local cache")
329        return row["id"]

SQLite-backed local mirror of paperless-ngx document metadata.

Wraps a ~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient and mirrors document metadata (plus tags, correspondents, document types, and storage paths) into a local SQLite database for fast, offline-capable search.

Sync is manual and explicit: call sync() to pull fresh data from the server. search_documents() is a pure SQLite query — no network is involved.

Example: async with PaperlessClient(url="...", api_key="...") as client: store = DocumentStore(client, "paperless.db") await store.sync() results = store.search_documents(tags=["invoice"], created_after="2024-01-01") store.close()

DocumentStore( client: PaperlessClient, db_path: str | pathlib._local.Path) 
87    def __init__(self, client: PaperlessClient, db_path: str | Path) -> None:
88        """Create a DocumentStore backed by a local SQLite file.
89
90        Args:
91            client: An initialised
92                :class:`~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient` used for
93                fetching data during :meth:`sync`.
94            db_path: Path to the SQLite database file.  The file is created
95                if it does not exist.
96        """
97        self._client = client
98        self._db_path = Path(db_path)
99        self._conn: sqlite3.Connection | None = None

Create a DocumentStore backed by a local SQLite file.

Args: client: An initialised ~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient used for fetching data during sync(). db_path: Path to the SQLite database file. The file is created if it does not exist.

def close(self) -> None: 
110    def close(self) -> None:
111        """Close the SQLite database connection."""
112        if self._conn is not None:
113            self._conn.close()
114            self._conn = None

Close the SQLite database connection.

async def sync(self) -> int: 
120    async def sync(self) -> int:
121        """Pull all documents and supporting metadata from the server and upsert locally.
122
123        Fetches documents, tags, correspondents, document types, and storage
124        paths in parallel, then upserts everything into SQLite.  Existing
125        local records are replaced; documents no longer present on the server
126        are *not* removed (incremental sync is not yet implemented).
127
128        Returns:
129            Number of documents synced.
130        """
131        logger.info("Starting sync from server")
132        docs, tags, correspondents, doc_types, storage_paths = await asyncio.gather(
133            self._client.list_documents(),
134            self._client.list_tags(),
135            self._client.list_correspondents(),
136            self._client.list_document_types(),
137            self._client.list_storage_paths(),
138        )
139
140        conn = self._get_conn()
141
142        # Upsert tags
143        conn.executemany(
144            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO tags (id, name, raw_json) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
145            [(t.id, t.name, t.model_dump_json()) for t in tags],
146        )
147
148        # Upsert correspondents
149        conn.executemany(
150            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO correspondents (id, name, raw_json) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
151            [(c.id, c.name, c.model_dump_json()) for c in correspondents],
152        )
153
154        # Upsert document types
155        conn.executemany(
156            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO document_types (id, name, raw_json) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
157            [(dt.id, dt.name, dt.model_dump_json()) for dt in doc_types],
158        )
159
160        # Upsert storage paths
161        conn.executemany(
162            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO storage_paths (id, name, raw_json) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
163            [(sp.id, sp.name, sp.model_dump_json()) for sp in storage_paths],
164        )
165
166        # Upsert documents
167        for doc in docs:
168            created_date_str = doc.created_date.isoformat() if doc.created_date else None
169            conn.execute(
170                """INSERT OR REPLACE INTO documents
171                   (id, title, correspondent_id, document_type_id, created_date, raw_json)
172                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
173                (
174                    doc.id,
175                    doc.title,
176                    doc.correspondent,
177                    doc.document_type,
178                    created_date_str,
179                    doc.model_dump_json(),
180                ),
181            )
182            # Rebuild document_tags
183            conn.execute("DELETE FROM document_tags WHERE document_id = ?", (doc.id,))
184            conn.executemany(
185                "INSERT OR IGNORE INTO document_tags (document_id, tag_id) VALUES (?, ?)",
186                [(doc.id, tag_id) for tag_id in doc.tags],
187            )
188
189        from datetime import datetime, timezone
190        conn.execute(
191            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO sync_metadata (key, value) VALUES ('last_sync', ?)",
192            (datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),),
193        )
194        conn.commit()
195        logger.info(
196            "Sync complete: %d documents, %d tags, %d correspondents, %d document_types, %d storage_paths",
197            len(docs), len(tags), len(correspondents), len(doc_types), len(storage_paths),
198        )
199
200        return len(docs)

Pull all documents and supporting metadata from the server and upsert locally.

Fetches documents, tags, correspondents, document types, and storage paths in parallel, then upserts everything into SQLite. Existing local records are replaced; documents no longer present on the server are not removed (incremental sync is not yet implemented).

Returns: Number of documents synced.

def search_documents( self, *, title_contains: str | None = None, title_regex: str | None = None, content_regex: str | None = None, tags: list[int | str] | None = None, created_after: str | None = None, created_before: str | None = None, correspondent: int | str | None = None) -> list[Document]: 
206    def search_documents(
207        self,
208        *,
209        title_contains: str | None = None,
210        title_regex: str | None = None,
211        content_regex: str | None = None,
212        tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
213        created_after: str | None = None,
214        created_before: str | None = None,
215        correspondent: int | str | None = None,
216    ) -> list[Document]:
217        """Search the local SQLite cache — no network request is made.
218
219        SQL filters (``title_contains``, ``created_after``, ``created_before``,
220        ``correspondent``, ``tags``) are applied first.  Regex filters
221        (``title_regex``, ``content_regex``) are then applied in Python on the
222        resulting rows.
223
224        Args:
225            title_contains: Case-insensitive substring match on the document
226                title.  Applied as a SQL ``LIKE`` filter.
227            title_regex: Python ``re`` pattern applied to the document title
228                (case-insensitive).  Applied after SQL filtering.
229            content_regex: Python ``re`` pattern applied to the OCR content of
230                each candidate document (case-insensitive).  Applied after SQL
231                filtering.  Content must have been present when :meth:`sync`
232                was last called.
233            tags: Documents must have **all** of these tags.  Accepts tag IDs
234                or tag names (resolved from the local cache).
235            created_after: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
236                documents created **after** this date are returned.
237            created_before: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
238                documents created **before** this date are returned.
239            correspondent: Filter to documents assigned to this correspondent.
240                Accepts a correspondent ID or name (resolved from the local
241                cache).
242
243        Returns:
244            List of :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`
245            objects matching all supplied filters.
246
247        Raises:
248            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If a tag or correspondent
249                name is supplied that does not exist in the local cache.
250        """
251        active_filters = {
252            k: v for k, v in {
253                "title_contains": title_contains,
254                "title_regex": title_regex,
255                "content_regex": content_regex,
256                "tags": tags,
257                "created_after": created_after,
258                "created_before": created_before,
259                "correspondent": correspondent,
260            }.items() if v is not None
261        }
262        if active_filters:
263            logger.debug(
264                "search_documents(%s)",
265                ", ".join(f"{k}={v!r}" for k, v in active_filters.items()),
266            )
267        else:
268            logger.debug("search_documents()")
269
270        conn = self._get_conn()
271
272        conditions: list[str] = []
273        bind: list[Any] = []
274
275        if title_contains is not None:
276            conditions.append("d.title LIKE ?")
277            bind.append(f"%{title_contains}%")
278
279        if created_after is not None:
280            conditions.append("d.created_date > ?")
281            bind.append(created_after)
282
283        if created_before is not None:
284            conditions.append("d.created_date < ?")
285            bind.append(created_before)
286
287        if correspondent is not None:
288            corr_id = self._resolve_local("correspondents", correspondent)
289            conditions.append("d.correspondent_id = ?")
290            bind.append(corr_id)
291
292        if tags:
293            tag_ids = [self._resolve_local("tags", t) for t in tags]
294            for tag_id in tag_ids:
295                conditions.append(
296                    "EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM document_tags dt WHERE dt.document_id = d.id AND dt.tag_id = ?)"
297                )
298                bind.append(tag_id)
299
300        where_clause = ("WHERE " + " AND ".join(conditions)) if conditions else ""
301        sql = f"SELECT d.raw_json FROM documents d {where_clause}"
302        rows = conn.execute(sql, bind).fetchall()
303
304        results: list[Document] = []
305        title_re = re.compile(title_regex, re.IGNORECASE) if title_regex else None
306        content_re = re.compile(content_regex, re.IGNORECASE) if content_regex else None
307
308        for row in rows:
309            data = json.loads(row["raw_json"])
310            if title_re and not title_re.search(data.get("title", "")):
311                continue
312            if content_re and not content_re.search(data.get("content", "") or ""):
313                continue
314            results.append(Document.model_validate(data))
315
316        logger.debug("search_documents returned %d result(s)", len(results))
317        return results

Search the local SQLite cache — no network request is made.

SQL filters (title_contains, created_after, created_before, correspondent, tags) are applied first. Regex filters (title_regex, content_regex) are then applied in Python on the resulting rows.

Args: title_contains: Case-insensitive substring match on the document title. Applied as a SQL LIKE filter. title_regex: Python re pattern applied to the document title (case-insensitive). Applied after SQL filtering. content_regex: Python re pattern applied to the OCR content of each candidate document (case-insensitive). Applied after SQL filtering. Content must have been present when sync() was last called. tags: Documents must have **all** of these tags. Accepts tag IDs or tag names (resolved from the local cache). created_after: ISO-8601 date string ("YYYY-MM-DD"). Only documents created after this date are returned. created_before: ISO-8601 date string ("YYYY-MM-DD"). Only documents created before this date are returned. correspondent: Filter to documents assigned to this correspondent. Accepts a correspondent ID or name (resolved from the local cache).

Returns: List of ~easypaperless.models.documents.Document objects matching all supplied filters.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If a tag or correspondent name is supplied that does not exist in the local cache.

class Correspondent(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
11class Correspondent(BaseModel):
12    """A paperless-ngx correspondent (sender or recipient of a document).
13
14    Attributes:
15        id: Unique correspondent ID.
16        name: Correspondent name.
17        document_count: Number of documents assigned to this correspondent.
18        last_correspondence: Date of the most recent document assigned here,
19            or ``None``.
20    """
21
22    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore")
23
24    id: int
25    name: str
26    slug: str | None = None
27    match: str | None = None
28    matching_algorithm: int | None = None
29    is_insensitive: bool | None = None
30    document_count: int | None = None
31    last_correspondence: date | None = None
32    owner: int | None = None
33    user_can_change: bool | None = None

A paperless-ngx correspondent (sender or recipient of a document).

Attributes: id: Unique correspondent ID. name: Correspondent name. document_count: Number of documents assigned to this correspondent. last_correspondence: Date of the most recent document assigned here, or None.

id: int = PydanticUndefined
name: str = PydanticUndefined
slug: str | None = None
match: str | None = None
matching_algorithm: int | None = None
is_insensitive: bool | None = None
document_count: int | None = None
last_correspondence: datetime.date | None = None
owner: int | None = None
user_can_change: bool | None = None
class CustomField(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
26class CustomField(BaseModel):
27    """A custom field definition in paperless-ngx.
28
29    Attributes:
30        id: Unique custom-field ID.
31        name: Field name shown in the UI.
32        data_type: The value type for this field (see
33            :class:`FieldDataType`).
34        extra_data: Additional configuration (e.g. select options), or
35            ``None``.
36        document_count: Number of documents that have a value for this field.
37    """
38
39    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore")
40
41    id: int
42    name: str
43    data_type: FieldDataType
44    extra_data: Any = None
45    document_count: int | None = None

A custom field definition in paperless-ngx.

Attributes: id: Unique custom-field ID. name: Field name shown in the UI. data_type: The value type for this field (see FieldDataType). extra_data: Additional configuration (e.g. select options), or None. document_count: Number of documents that have a value for this field.

id: int = PydanticUndefined
name: str = PydanticUndefined
data_type: FieldDataType = PydanticUndefined
extra_data: Any = None
document_count: int | None = None
class CustomFieldValue(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
66class CustomFieldValue(BaseModel):
67    """A custom field value attached to a document.
68
69    Attributes:
70        field: ID of the :class:`~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField`
71            definition.
72        value: The stored value; its Python type depends on the field's
73            ``data_type``.
74    """
75
76    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore")
77
78    field: int
79    value: Any = None

A custom field value attached to a document.

Attributes: field: ID of the ~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField definition. value: The stored value; its Python type depends on the field's data_type.

field: int = PydanticUndefined
value: Any = None
class Document(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
165class Document(BaseModel):
166    """A paperless-ngx document.
167
168    Attributes:
169        id: Unique document ID.
170        title: Document title.
171        content: Full OCR text content, if available.
172        tags: List of tag IDs assigned to this document.
173        document_type: ID of the assigned document type, or ``None``.
174        correspondent: ID of the assigned correspondent, or ``None``.
175        storage_path: ID of the assigned storage path, or ``None``.
176        created: Full creation datetime.
177        created_date: Date portion of creation (``date`` object).
178        archive_serial_number: Archive serial number (ASN), or ``None``.
179        custom_fields: List of :class:`CustomFieldValue` instances.
180        notes: User notes attached to this document.
181        search_hit: Full-text search relevance metadata, populated only when
182            the document was returned by a full-text search.
183        metadata: Extended file-level metadata (checksums, sizes, MIME type).
184            ``None`` unless the document was fetched with
185            ``include_metadata=True`` or enriched via
186            :meth:`~easypaperless.PaperlessClient.get_document_metadata`.
187    """
188
189    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
190
191    id: int
192    title: str
193    content: str | None = None
194    tags: list[int] = Field(default_factory=list)
195    document_type: int | None = None
196    correspondent: int | None = None
197    storage_path: int | None = None
198    created: datetime | None = None
199    created_date: date | None = None
200    modified: datetime | None = None
201    added: datetime | None = None
202    archive_serial_number: int | None = None
203    original_file_name: str | None = None
204    archived_file_name: str | None = None
205    owner: int | None = None
206    user_can_change: bool | None = None
207    is_shared_by_requester: bool | None = None
208    notes: list[DocumentNote] = Field(default_factory=list)
209    custom_fields: list[CustomFieldValue] = Field(default_factory=list)
210    search_hit: SearchHit | None = Field(default=None, alias="__search_hit__")
211    metadata: DocumentMetadata | None = None

A paperless-ngx document.

Attributes: id: Unique document ID. title: Document title. content: Full OCR text content, if available. tags: List of tag IDs assigned to this document. document_type: ID of the assigned document type, or None. correspondent: ID of the assigned correspondent, or None. storage_path: ID of the assigned storage path, or None. created: Full creation datetime. created_date: Date portion of creation (date object). archive_serial_number: Archive serial number (ASN), or None. custom_fields: List of CustomFieldValue instances. notes: User notes attached to this document. search_hit: Full-text search relevance metadata, populated only when the document was returned by a full-text search. metadata: Extended file-level metadata (checksums, sizes, MIME type). None unless the document was fetched with include_metadata=True or enriched via ~easypaperless.PaperlessClient.get_document_metadata().

id: int = PydanticUndefined
title: str = PydanticUndefined
content: str | None = None
tags: list[int] = PydanticUndefined
document_type: int | None = None
correspondent: int | None = None
storage_path: int | None = None
created: datetime.datetime | None = None
created_date: datetime.date | None = None
modified: datetime.datetime | None = None
added: datetime.datetime | None = None
archive_serial_number: int | None = None
original_file_name: str | None = None
archived_file_name: str | None = None
owner: int | None = None
user_can_change: bool | None = None
is_shared_by_requester: bool | None = None
notes: list[DocumentNote] = PydanticUndefined
custom_fields: list[CustomFieldValue] = PydanticUndefined
search_hit: SearchHit | None = None
metadata: DocumentMetadata | None = None
class DocumentMetadata(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
124class DocumentMetadata(BaseModel):
125    """Extended file-level metadata for a document.
126
127    Returned by ``GET /api/documents/{id}/metadata/`` and optionally attached
128    to a :class:`Document` when :meth:`~easypaperless.PaperlessClient.get_document`
129    is called with ``include_metadata=True``.
130
131    Because reading metadata requires disk I/O it is **not** included in
132    document list responses; it must be requested explicitly.
133
134    Attributes:
135        original_checksum: MD5 checksum of the original uploaded file.
136        original_size: Size of the original file in bytes.
137        original_mime_type: MIME type of the original file
138            (e.g. ``"application/pdf"``).
139        media_filename: Path of the archived file relative to the
140            paperless-ngx media root.
141        has_archive_version: ``True`` when paperless-ngx has produced an
142            archived (post-processed) PDF in addition to the original.
143        original_metadata: File-level metadata entries extracted from the
144            original document (PDF XMP/info tags, etc.).
145        archive_checksum: MD5 checksum of the archived file, or ``None`` if
146            no archive version exists.
147        archive_size: Size of the archived file in bytes, or ``None``.
148        archive_metadata: File-level metadata entries from the archived
149            document, or ``None``.
150    """
151
152    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore")
153
154    original_checksum: str | None = None
155    original_size: int | None = None
156    original_mime_type: str | None = None
157    media_filename: str | None = None
158    has_archive_version: bool | None = None
159    original_metadata: list[FileMetadataEntry] = Field(default_factory=list)
160    archive_checksum: str | None = None
161    archive_size: int | None = None
162    archive_metadata: list[FileMetadataEntry] | None = None

Extended file-level metadata for a document.

Returned by GET /api/documents/{id}/metadata/ and optionally attached to a Document when ~easypaperless.PaperlessClient.get_document() is called with include_metadata=True.

Because reading metadata requires disk I/O it is not included in document list responses; it must be requested explicitly.

Attributes: original_checksum: MD5 checksum of the original uploaded file. original_size: Size of the original file in bytes. original_mime_type: MIME type of the original file (e.g. "application/pdf"). media_filename: Path of the archived file relative to the paperless-ngx media root. has_archive_version: True when paperless-ngx has produced an archived (post-processed) PDF in addition to the original. original_metadata: File-level metadata entries extracted from the original document (PDF XMP/info tags, etc.). archive_checksum: MD5 checksum of the archived file, or None if no archive version exists. archive_size: Size of the archived file in bytes, or None. archive_metadata: File-level metadata entries from the archived document, or None.

original_checksum: str | None = None
original_size: int | None = None
original_mime_type: str | None = None
media_filename: str | None = None
has_archive_version: bool | None = None
original_metadata: list[FileMetadataEntry] = PydanticUndefined
archive_checksum: str | None = None
archive_size: int | None = None
archive_metadata: list[FileMetadataEntry] | None = None
class DocumentNote(pydantic.main.BaseModel):

89        document: ID of the parent document.
90        user: ID of the user who created the note.
91    """
92
93    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore")
94
95    id: int | None = None
96    note: str
97    created: datetime | None = None
98    document: int | None = None
99    user: int | None = None

A user note attached to a document.

Attributes: id: Unique note ID. note: Text content of the note. created: Timestamp when the note was created. document: ID of the parent document. user: ID of the user who created the note.

note: str = PydanticUndefined
created: datetime.datetime | None = None
document: int | None = None
user: int | None = None

class FieldDataType(enum.StrEnum): 
12class FieldDataType(StrEnum):
13    """Allowed data types for a custom field."""
14
15    string = "string"
16    boolean = "boolean"
17    integer = "integer"
18    float = "float"
19    monetary = "monetary"
20    date = "date"
21    url = "url"
22    documentlink = "documentlink"
23    select = "select"

Allowed data types for a custom field.

string = <FieldDataType.string: 'string'>
boolean = <FieldDataType.boolean: 'boolean'>
integer = <FieldDataType.integer: 'integer'>
float = <FieldDataType.float: 'float'>
monetary = <FieldDataType.monetary: 'monetary'>
date = <FieldDataType.date: 'date'>
url = <FieldDataType.url: 'url'>
documentlink = <FieldDataType.documentlink: 'documentlink'>
select = <FieldDataType.select: 'select'>
class FileMetadataEntry(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
102class FileMetadataEntry(BaseModel):
103    """A single embedded metadata key-value pair from a document file.
104
105    Paperless-ngx reads file-level metadata (e.g. PDF XMP/info tags) and
106    exposes each entry in this format.  ``namespace`` and ``prefix`` are
107    ``None`` for non-namespaced entries.
108
109    Attributes:
110        namespace: XML namespace URI, or ``None``.
111        prefix: Namespace prefix (e.g. ``"pdf"``), or ``None``.
112        key: Metadata key (e.g. ``"Producer"``).
113        value: Metadata value as a string.
114    """
115
116    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore")
117
118    namespace: str | None = None
119    prefix: str | None = None
120    key: str
121    value: str

A single embedded metadata key-value pair from a document file.

Paperless-ngx reads file-level metadata (e.g. PDF XMP/info tags) and exposes each entry in this format. namespace and prefix are None for non-namespaced entries.

Attributes: namespace: XML namespace URI, or None. prefix: Namespace prefix (e.g. "pdf"), or None. key: Metadata key (e.g. "Producer"). value: Metadata value as a string.

namespace: str | None = None
prefix: str | None = None
key: str = PydanticUndefined
value: str = PydanticUndefined
class SearchHit(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
49class SearchHit(BaseModel):
50    """Full-text search relevance metadata returned alongside a document.
51
52    Attributes:
53        score: Relevance score assigned by the Whoosh FTS engine.
54        highlights: HTML snippet with matching terms highlighted.
55        rank: Position in the result set by relevance.
56    """
57
58    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore")
59
60    score: float | None = None
61    highlights: str | None = None
62    note_highlights: str | None = None
63    rank: int | None = None

Full-text search relevance metadata returned alongside a document.

Attributes: score: Relevance score assigned by the Whoosh FTS engine. highlights: HTML snippet with matching terms highlighted. rank: Position in the result set by relevance.

score: float | None = None
highlights: str | None = None
note_highlights: str | None = None
rank: int | None = None
class StoragePath(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
 9class StoragePath(BaseModel):
10    """A paperless-ngx storage path — controls where archived files are stored.
11
12    Attributes:
13        id: Unique storage-path ID.
14        name: Storage-path name.
15        path: Template string used to build the storage path, e.g.
16            ``"{created_year}/{correspondent}/{title}"``.
17        document_count: Number of documents using this storage path.
18    """
19
20    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore")
21
22    id: int
23    name: str
24    slug: str | None = None
25    path: str | None = None
26    match: str | None = None
27    matching_algorithm: int | None = None
28    is_insensitive: bool | None = None
29    document_count: int | None = None
30    owner: int | None = None
31    user_can_change: bool | None = None

A paperless-ngx storage path — controls where archived files are stored.

Attributes: id: Unique storage-path ID. name: Storage-path name. path: Template string used to build the storage path, e.g. "{created_year}/{correspondent}/{title}". document_count: Number of documents using this storage path.

id: int = PydanticUndefined
name: str = PydanticUndefined
slug: str | None = None
path: str | None = None
match: str | None = None
matching_algorithm: int | None = None
is_insensitive: bool | None = None
document_count: int | None = None
owner: int | None = None
user_can_change: bool | None = None
class Tag(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
 9class Tag(BaseModel):
10    """A paperless-ngx tag.
11
12    Attributes:
13        id: Unique tag ID.
14        name: Tag name.
15        color: Hex colour code used in the UI (e.g. ``"#ff0000"``).
16        is_inbox_tag: If ``True``, newly ingested documents receive this tag
17            automatically until they are processed.
18        document_count: Number of documents currently assigned to this tag.
19        parent: ID of the parent tag for hierarchical tag trees, or ``None``.
20        children: IDs of child tags, or ``None``.
21    """
22
23    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore")
24
25    id: int
26    name: str
27    slug: str | None = None
28    color: str | None = None
29    text_color: str | None = None
30    match: str | None = None
31    matching_algorithm: int | None = None
32    is_insensitive: bool | None = None
33    is_inbox_tag: bool | None = None
34    document_count: int | None = None
35    owner: int | None = None
36    user_can_change: bool | None = None
37    parent: int | None = None
38    children: list[int] | None = None

A paperless-ngx tag.

Attributes: id: Unique tag ID. name: Tag name. color: Hex colour code used in the UI (e.g. "#ff0000"). is_inbox_tag: If True, newly ingested documents receive this tag automatically until they are processed. document_count: Number of documents currently assigned to this tag. parent: ID of the parent tag for hierarchical tag trees, or None. children: IDs of child tags, or None.

id: int = PydanticUndefined
name: str = PydanticUndefined
slug: str | None = None
color: str | None = None
text_color: str | None = None
match: str | None = None
matching_algorithm: int | None = None
is_insensitive: bool | None = None
is_inbox_tag: bool | None = None
document_count: int | None = None
owner: int | None = None
user_can_change: bool | None = None
parent: int | None = None
children: list[int] | None = None
class Task(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
24class Task(BaseModel):
25    """A paperless-ngx background processing task (e.g. document ingestion).
26
27    Attributes:
28        task_id: Unique Celery task identifier.
29        task_file_name: Original file name submitted for processing.
30        status: Current task status as a :class:`TaskStatus` enum value.
31        result: Human-readable result or error message, set on completion.
32        related_document: String representation of the resulting document ID
33            on success, ``None`` otherwise.
34    """
35
36    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore")
37
38    task_id: str
39    task_file_name: str | None = None
40    date_created: datetime | None = None
41    date_done: datetime | None = None
42    type: str | None = None
43    status: TaskStatus | None = None
44    result: str | None = None
45    acknowledged: bool | None = None
46    related_document: str | None = None

A paperless-ngx background processing task (e.g. document ingestion).

Attributes: task_id: Unique Celery task identifier. task_file_name: Original file name submitted for processing. status: Current task status as a TaskStatus enum value. result: Human-readable result or error message, set on completion. related_document: String representation of the resulting document ID on success, None otherwise.

task_id: str = PydanticUndefined
task_file_name: str | None = None
date_created: datetime.datetime | None = None
date_done: datetime.datetime | None = None
type: str | None = None
status: TaskStatus | None = None
result: str | None = None
acknowledged: bool | None = None
related_document: str | None = None
class TaskStatus(enum.StrEnum): 
13class TaskStatus(StrEnum):
14    """Status values for a paperless-ngx background processing task."""
15
16    PENDING = "PENDING"
17    STARTED = "STARTED"
18    SUCCESS = "SUCCESS"
19    FAILURE = "FAILURE"
20    RETRY = "RETRY"
21    REVOKED = "REVOKED"

Status values for a paperless-ngx background processing task.

PENDING = <TaskStatus.PENDING: 'PENDING'>
STARTED = <TaskStatus.STARTED: 'STARTED'>
SUCCESS = <TaskStatus.SUCCESS: 'SUCCESS'>
FAILURE = <TaskStatus.FAILURE: 'FAILURE'>
RETRY = <TaskStatus.RETRY: 'RETRY'>
REVOKED = <TaskStatus.REVOKED: 'REVOKED'>
class PaperlessError(builtins.Exception): 
 7class PaperlessError(Exception):
 8    """Base exception for all easypaperless errors.
 9
10    Attributes:
11        status_code: The HTTP status code associated with the error, or
12            ``None`` for non-HTTP errors (e.g. timeouts, local validation).
13    """
14
15    def __init__(self, message: str, status_code: int | None = None) -> None:
16        """Create a PaperlessError.
17
18        Args:
19            message: Human-readable error description.
20            status_code: HTTP status code, if applicable.
21        """
22        super().__init__(message)
23        self.status_code = status_code

Base exception for all easypaperless errors.

Attributes: status_code: The HTTP status code associated with the error, or None for non-HTTP errors (e.g. timeouts, local validation).

PaperlessError(message: str, status_code: int | None = None) 
15    def __init__(self, message: str, status_code: int | None = None) -> None:
16        """Create a PaperlessError.
17
18        Args:
19            message: Human-readable error description.
20            status_code: HTTP status code, if applicable.
21        """
22        super().__init__(message)
23        self.status_code = status_code

Create a PaperlessError.

Args: message: Human-readable error description. status_code: HTTP status code, if applicable.

status_code
class AuthError(easypaperless.PaperlessError): 
26class AuthError(PaperlessError):
27    """Raised on 401 or 403 responses.
28
29    Indicates that the API key is missing, invalid, or lacks permission for
30    the requested operation.
31    """

Raised on 401 or 403 responses.

Indicates that the API key is missing, invalid, or lacks permission for the requested operation.

class NotFoundError(easypaperless.PaperlessError): 
34class NotFoundError(PaperlessError):
35    """Raised on 404 responses or when a local name lookup fails.
36
37    Also raised by
38    :meth:`~easypaperless.store.DocumentStore.search_documents` when a tag
39    or correspondent name is not found in the local SQLite cache.
40    """

Raised on 404 responses or when a local name lookup fails.

Also raised by ~easypaperless.store.DocumentStore.search_documents() when a tag or correspondent name is not found in the local SQLite cache.

class ValidationError(easypaperless.PaperlessError): 
43class ValidationError(PaperlessError):
44    """Raised on 422 responses or bad input supplied by the caller."""

Raised on 422 responses or bad input supplied by the caller.

class ServerError(easypaperless.PaperlessError): 
47class ServerError(PaperlessError):
48    """Raised on 5xx responses or unrecoverable transport errors."""

Raised on 5xx responses or unrecoverable transport errors.

class UploadError(easypaperless.PaperlessError): 
51class UploadError(PaperlessError):
52    """Raised when file submission or document processing fails.
53
54    Typically raised when paperless-ngx reports a ``FAILURE`` status on the
55    Celery task created by
56    :meth:`~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient.upload_document`.
57    """

Raised when file submission or document processing fails.

Typically raised when paperless-ngx reports a FAILURE status on the Celery task created by ~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient.upload_document().

class TaskTimeoutError(easypaperless.PaperlessError): 
60class TaskTimeoutError(PaperlessError):
61    """Raised when upload polling exceeds the configured timeout.
62
63    Raised by :meth:`~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient.upload_document`
64    (with ``wait=True``) when the document processing task does not reach a
65    terminal state within ``poll_timeout`` seconds.
66    """
67
68    def __init__(self, message: str) -> None:
69        super().__init__(message, status_code=None)

Raised when upload polling exceeds the configured timeout.

Raised by ~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient.upload_document() (with wait=True) when the document processing task does not reach a terminal state within poll_timeout seconds.

TaskTimeoutError(message: str) 
68    def __init__(self, message: str) -> None:
69        super().__init__(message, status_code=None)

Create a PaperlessError.

Args: message: Human-readable error description. status_code: HTTP status code, if applicable.
